roadmap_checkpoints.label: HTML5 Foundations
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Master the building blocks of the web. You'll learn how browsers parse HTML, semantic element choices that affect SEO and accessibility, and how to structure a document that's both human-readable and machine-friendly.
learning_goals: Understand the role of semantic HTML elements (header, main, article, section)
Build well-structured forms with proper labels and input types
Apply accessibility best practices using ARIA roles and alt text
Validate HTML using the W3C validator and fix common errors

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Advanced CSS & Grid
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Go beyond basic styling. This checkpoint covers CSS Grid and Flexbox layouts, responsive design with media queries, CSS custom properties (variables), and modern techniques like clamp() and container queries.
learning_goals: Build complex two-dimensional layouts using CSS Grid
Create flexible, one-dimensional layouts with Flexbox
Write responsive styles using media queries and fluid units
Use CSS custom properties to build a maintainable design token system

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Layouts Master Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Test your knowledge of HTML structure and CSS layouts. This quiz covers semantic markup, Flexbox, Grid, and responsive design patterns encountered in real-world projects.
learning_goals: Recall the difference between block, inline, and inline-block elements
Identify correct Grid and Flexbox properties for given layout challenges
Diagnose and fix common layout bugs
Apply media query breakpoints to a given design spec

roadmap_checkpoints.label: JS Logic & Variables
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Learn the fundamentals of JavaScript: data types, variable scoping (var vs let vs const), operators, control flow, and functions. This is the foundation for all JS-powered interactivity.
learning_goals: Distinguish between let, const, and var and when to use each
Write functions using both declaration and arrow-function syntax
Control program flow using if/else, switch, and ternary expressions
Understand truthy/falsy values and short-circuit evaluation

roadmap_checkpoints.label: DOM & Events
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Bring web pages to life by manipulating the DOM and responding to user events. Covers querySelector, event listeners, event propagation, and the difference between innerHTML and textContent.
learning_goals: Select and modify DOM elements using querySelector and querySelectorAll
Attach and remove event listeners for user interactions
Explain event bubbling and capture, and use stopPropagation when needed
Dynamically create and remove elements from the page

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Interactive Site Project
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Apply everything learned so far to build a small interactive website — a to-do list, a quiz app, or a weather dashboard. Focus on clean HTML structure, responsive CSS, and vanilla JS logic with no frameworks.
learning_goals: Plan a small project by breaking it into HTML structure, CSS styles, and JS logic
Implement CRUD operations (add, edit, delete items) using DOM manipulation
Persist data across page reloads using localStorage
Deploy the project to GitHub Pages or Netlify

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Asynchronous JS
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Understand how JavaScript handles async operations: the event loop, callbacks, Promises, and async/await syntax. Learn to fetch data from APIs and handle loading and error states gracefully.
learning_goals: Explain the JavaScript event loop and call stack
Chain Promises using .then()/.catch() and convert to async/await
Fetch data from a public REST API and render it in the DOM
Handle network errors and display appropriate user feedback

roadmap_checkpoints.label: React Fundamentals
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Introduction to React: JSX syntax, functional components, props, and the useState and useEffect hooks. Build a small component-driven UI and understand the virtual DOM and reconciliation.
learning_goals: Write functional components using JSX and pass data via props
Manage local component state with the useState hook
Trigger side effects and data fetching with useEffect
Compose a multi-component page with a clear data-flow hierarchy

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Programming Fundamentals
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson introduces the foundational concepts of programming, including variables, data types, control structures, and functions. It's crucial for understanding how software works and for scripting security tools.
learning_goals: Understand basic programming constructs like loops and conditionals.
Identify and use different data types effectively.
Write simple scripts to automate tasks.
Comprehend the logic behind program execution.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Fundamental IT Skills
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module covers essential IT knowledge required for any technical role, including basic computer operations, file system navigation, and common software usage. It forms the bedrock for more specialized security concepts.
learning_goals: Navigate file systems using both GUI and command line interfaces.
Perform basic software installation and configuration.
Understand fundamental concepts of computer operation.
Manage files and directories efficiently.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Programming Fundamentals + Fundamental IT Skills Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your foundational understanding of core programming principles and essential IT operational skills. It ensures you have a solid groundwork before advancing to more complex technical and security topics.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on programming fundamentals (syntax, logic, data types).
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of basic IT skills (OS navigation, file management).
Identify key concepts related to computer operation.
Achieve a passing score to progress to advanced modules.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Computer Hardware Components
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson provides an in-depth look at the various internal and external components that make up a computer system. Understanding hardware is critical for diagnosing issues and identifying potential vulnerabilities.
learning_goals: Identify major internal computer components (CPU, RAM, motherboard, storage).
Understand the function of each hardware component.
Recognize common peripheral devices and their roles.
Differentiate between various types of storage and memory.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Connection Types and their function
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module explores different types of physical and wireless connections used in computing and networking, explaining their purpose and how they facilitate data transfer. Knowledge of connection types is vital for network troubleshooting and security.
learning_goals: Identify common physical connection types (e.g., USB, HDMI, Ethernet).
Understand the function and typical uses of each connection type.
Differentiate between wired and wireless communication methods.
Recognize the appropriate connection for various data transfer needs.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: OS-Independent Troubleshooting
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson teaches systematic troubleshooting methodologies that are applicable regardless of the operating system. Developing these skills is essential for efficiently resolving technical problems and security incidents.
learning_goals: Apply a systematic approach to diagnose technical problems.
Identify common troubleshooting steps (e.g., isolate, test, document).
Resolve basic hardware and software issues without OS-specific knowledge.
Utilize diagnostic tools effectively.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: CompTIA Linux+
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This course covers advanced Linux system administration, including command-line tools, scripting, networking, and security concepts within the Linux environment. It is a key certification for professionals working with Linux systems.
learning_goals: Master advanced Linux command-line operations and scripting.
Manage Linux users, groups, and permissions.
Configure and troubleshoot Linux networking services.
Implement basic security measures on Linux systems.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Computer Hardware Components + Connection Types and their function + OS-Independent Troubleshooting + CompTIA Linux+ Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz evaluates your understanding of computer hardware, various connection types, general troubleshooting techniques, and essential Linux system administration. It confirms your readiness for more advanced technical and security topics.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on computer hardware and their functions.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of different connection types and their uses.
Apply OS-independent troubleshooting methodologies to various scenarios.
Exhibit proficiency in CompTIA Linux+ concepts, including administration and security.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Understand Basics of Popular Suites
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module introduces the functionalities and common uses of popular office and productivity suites, such as Microsoft Office and Google Workspace. Understanding these tools is important as they are frequently targeted in social engineering attacks.
learning_goals: Identify the core applications within popular software suites (e.g., Word, Excel, Gmail).
Understand the basic functionalities of word processors, spreadsheets, and presentation tools.
Recognize collaboration features within cloud-based suites.
Familiarize yourself with common productivity workflows.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: CCNA
roadmap_checkpoints.description: The CCNA certification curriculum provides a comprehensive understanding of networking fundamentals, including LANs, WANs, routing, switching, and network security principles. It's a cornerstone for any network-focused security engineer.
learning_goals: Configure and troubleshoot Cisco routers and switches.
Understand network protocols like TCP/IP, OSPF, and EIGRP.
Implement basic network security measures.
Design and verify small to medium-sized networks.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Basics of Computer Networking
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers the foundational concepts of how computers communicate, including the OSI model, TCP/IP stack, IP addressing, and common network services. A strong grasp of networking is indispensable for security engineers.
learning_goals: Understand the layers and functions of the OSI and TCP/IP models.
Explain IP addressing, subnetting, and routing concepts.
Identify common network protocols (HTTP, DNS, DHCP) and their roles.
Differentiate between various network topologies.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Security Engineer Project #1
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This project challenges you to set up a small, secure virtual network using virtualization software, configuring basic services and network segmentation. It's designed to apply foundational IT and networking skills in a practical security context.
learning_goals: Successfully install and configure virtualization software (e.g., VirtualBox, VMware).
Deploy and configure multiple virtual machines (e.g., Linux, Windows).
Implement basic network segmentation (e.g., VLANs) within the virtual environment.
Demonstrate secure configuration of network services (e.g., SSH, basic firewall rules).

roadmap_checkpoints.label: CISA
roadmap_checkpoints.description: The CISA certification focuses on auditing, control, and assurance of information systems. It equips security engineers with the knowledge to identify and assess vulnerabilities, ensure compliance, and manage IT risks effectively.
learning_goals: Understand the process of auditing information systems.
Identify and assess IT governance and management frameworks.
Evaluate information systems acquisition, development, and implementation.
Analyze information security operations and business resilience.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: CISM
roadmap_checkpoints.description: The CISM certification targets information security management, focusing on governance, program development, incident management, and risk management. It's crucial for engineers aspiring to leadership roles in security.
learning_goals: Develop and manage information security governance frameworks.
Design and implement information security programs.
Oversee incident management and response activities.
Manage information security risks and compliance.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: CISA + CISM Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz rigorously tests your knowledge of information systems auditing (CISA) and information security management (CISM) principles. It validates your understanding of governance, risk, compliance, and incident response from a management perspective.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on IT governance, risk management, and compliance.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of information security program development and management.
Identify best practices in information systems auditing.
Achieve a passing score on both CISA and CISM related topics.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: GPEN
roadmap_checkpoints.description: The GPEN certification from SANS GIAC focuses on practical penetration testing skills across various systems and networks. It teaches ethical hacking techniques to identify and exploit vulnerabilities.
learning_goals: Perform reconnaissance and vulnerability scanning.
Execute common network and system exploitation techniques.
Understand post-exploitation strategies like privilege escalation.
Generate comprehensive penetration test reports.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: GWAPT
roadmap_checkpoints.description: The GWAPT certification specializes in web application penetration testing, covering common vulnerabilities like those outlined in OWASP Top 10. This is essential for securing modern web-based services.
learning_goals: Identify and exploit common web application vulnerabilities (e.g., SQL Injection, XSS).
Understand authentication and session management weaknesses.
Utilize web application penetration testing tools effectively.
Report findings and recommend remediation strategies for web applications.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Basics of Subnetting
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson provides a fundamental understanding of subnetting, which is the practice of dividing a network into smaller, more manageable subnetworks. It's a critical skill for network design, security, and IP address management.
learning_goals: Calculate subnet masks and network addresses.
Determine the number of usable hosts and subnets from a given IP address range.
Understand the benefits of subnetting for network organization and security.
Apply subnetting concepts to real-world network design scenarios.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Operating Systems
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module covers the core concepts and architectures of various operating systems, including Windows, Linux, and macOS. Understanding how OSes function is fundamental to securing them against attacks.
learning_goals: Differentiate between major operating system types and their core functionalities.
Understand user and kernel mode operations.
Identify basic file system structures and process management.
Recognize common OS components and services.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: GPEN + GWAPT + Basics of Subnetting + Operating Systems Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz rigorously evaluates your practical penetration testing abilities for both networks (GPEN) and web applications (GWAPT), alongside your understanding of subnetting and operating system principles. It confirms readiness for advanced offensive security challenges.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on penetration testing methodologies and tools.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of web application vulnerabilities and exploits.
Apply subnetting calculations and principles to network scenarios.
Exhibit strong understanding of operating system architectures and security implications.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: OSCP
roadmap_checkpoints.description: The OSCP certification is a highly practical and challenging penetration testing certification that requires candidates to compromise a series of live machines in a lab environment. It emphasizes hands-on exploitation skills.
learning_goals: Perform advanced reconnaissance and enumeration against target systems.
Identify and exploit complex vulnerabilities using manual techniques.
Execute privilege escalation and lateral movement within compromised networks.
Document the penetration testing process and findings accurately.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: CREST
roadmap_checkpoints.description: CREST is an international not-for-profit accreditation body for information security companies and individuals. This module introduces the standards and methodologies promoted by CREST for ethical hacking and security testing services.
learning_goals: Understand the ethical guidelines and professional standards for security testing.
Familiarize yourself with CREST's accreditation schemes and certifications.
Recognize the importance of scope, consent, and reporting in penetration testing.
Comprehend the different types of security testing services endorsed by CREST.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Public vs Private IP Addresses
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson clarifies the distinction between public and private IP addresses, their usage, and how Network Address Translation (NAT) facilitates communication between them. This is crucial for understanding internet connectivity and network segmentation.
learning_goals: Differentiate between public and private IP address ranges.
Explain the purpose and function of private IP addresses within local networks.
Understand how public IP addresses enable global internet access.
Describe the role of NAT in translating between private and public IP addresses.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Security Engineer Project #2
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This project tasks you with performing a simulated penetration test on a provided vulnerable system or network. You will identify vulnerabilities, exploit them to gain access, and document your findings, mirroring the OSCP approach.
learning_goals: Apply penetration testing methodologies (reconnaissance, scanning, exploitation).
Utilize common penetration testing tools and techniques.
Identify and exploit real-world vulnerabilities in a controlled environment.
Create a professional penetration test report detailing findings and recommendations.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Installation and Configuration
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module covers the practical aspects of installing operating systems and configuring software and services. Proper installation and configuration are foundational for building secure systems.
learning_goals: Perform a clean installation of various operating systems (e.g., Windows, Linux).
Configure essential OS settings and network parameters.
Install and update software applications securely.
Understand the importance of secure default configurations.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: loopback
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson explains the concept of a loopback interface, a virtual network interface that acts as a self-contained communication channel within a single device. It's fundamental for testing network services locally.
learning_goals: Define what a loopback interface is and its primary purpose.
Understand how services can communicate with themselves via loopback.
Identify the standard loopback IP address (127.0.0.1/::1).
Utilize the loopback interface for local network testing and troubleshooting.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: CIDR
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson delves into Classless Inter-Domain Routing (CIDR), a method for allocating IP addresses and routing IP packets more efficiently. Understanding CIDR is essential for modern network design and security.
learning_goals: Explain the purpose and benefits of CIDR over classful addressing.
Calculate network and host addresses using CIDR notation.
Understand how CIDR blocks are used for route aggregation.
Apply CIDR concepts to optimize IP address allocation and routing.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Installation and Configuration + loopback + CIDR Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your understanding of operating system installation, configuration best practices, and crucial networking concepts like loopback interfaces and CIDR. It ensures you can set up and understand modern network addressing.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on OS installation and secure configuration.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of loopback interface functionality.
Apply CIDR notation to calculate network addresses and subnets.
Achieve a passing score to progress in network and system management.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: localhost
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson explains the 'localhost' hostname and its association with the loopback IP address. It's a fundamental concept for understanding local network communication and testing applications.
learning_goals: Define 'localhost' and its relationship to the loopback IP address.
Understand why 'localhost' is used for local service access.
Explain how applications bind to localhost for testing.
Differentiate between accessing services via localhost versus a local network IP.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: VMWare
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module provides an introduction to VMware, a leading virtualization platform, covering its components, features, and deployment of virtual machines. Proficiency in virtualization is crucial for lab environments and cloud infrastructure.
learning_goals: Understand the core concepts of virtualization and hypervisors.
Install and configure VMware Workstation or ESXi.
Create and manage virtual machines within VMware.
Configure virtual networks and storage in a VMware environment.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: localhost + VMWare Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz evaluates your understanding of 'localhost' as a fundamental networking concept and your proficiency with VMware virtualization software. It ensures you can effectively work with virtual environments for testing and development.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on the 'localhost' concept and its applications.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of VMware installation and VM management.
Understand virtual networking within a VMware setup.
Achieve a passing score to proceed with virtualization and system configuration.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: VirtualBox
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson introduces VirtualBox, a popular open-source virtualization software, covering its installation, configuration, and management of virtual machines. It provides an alternative to VMware for creating lab environments.
learning_goals: Install and configure Oracle VirtualBox on a host operating system.
Create, manage, and clone virtual machines using VirtualBox.
Configure virtual network adapters and storage for VMs.
Utilize VirtualBox features like snapshots and shared folders.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Different Versions and Differences
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module explores the different versions of operating systems and major software applications, highlighting their key features, compatibility, and security implications. Understanding version differences is vital for patch management and vulnerability assessment.
learning_goals: Identify various versions of popular operating systems (e.g., Windows 10 vs. 11, different Linux distros).
Understand the key feature and performance differences between software versions.
Recognize how versioning impacts compatibility and security updates.
Explain the importance of keeping software updated for security reasons.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: subnet mask
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson details the function of a subnet mask, which is used to divide an IP address into network and host portions. A clear understanding of subnet masks is fundamental for networking and IP address management.
learning_goals: Explain the purpose and structure of a subnet mask.
Identify the network and host portions of an IP address using a subnet mask.
Perform binary conversions to understand subnet mask calculations.
Apply subnet masks to correctly configure network interfaces.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: esxi
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module introduces VMware ESXi, a bare-metal hypervisor, explaining its architecture, installation, and management for enterprise-level virtualization. It provides a deeper dive into server virtualization concepts.
learning_goals: Understand the architecture and benefits of a bare-metal hypervisor like ESXi.
Perform a basic installation and initial configuration of ESXi.
Manage virtual machines and virtual networks on an ESXi host.
Differentiate between ESXi and client-side virtualization platforms (e.g., Workstation, VirtualBox).

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Security Engineer Project #3
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This project involves deploying and securing multiple virtual machines across different virtualization platforms (e.g., VMware and VirtualBox), then configuring their networking to mimic a small corporate environment. You'll demonstrate proficiency in hypervisor management and network design.
learning_goals: Successfully install and configure multiple hypervisors (e.g., VMware ESXi, VirtualBox).
Deploy and manage diverse operating systems as guest VMs.
Configure complex virtual network settings, including subnetting and routing.
Document the virtual lab setup and justify security decisions.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: proxmox
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson introduces Proxmox VE, an open-source virtualization platform that combines KVM and LXC. It covers its installation, management, and features, offering an alternative for robust server virtualization.
learning_goals: Understand the core features and benefits of Proxmox VE.
Perform a basic installation and initial configuration of Proxmox.
Create and manage virtual machines (KVM) and containers (LXC).
Configure storage and network settings within the Proxmox environment.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Navigating using GUI and CLI
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module focuses on mastering navigation and interaction with operating systems using both Graphical User Interfaces (GUI) and Command Line Interfaces (CLI). Proficiency in both is essential for effective system administration and security tasks.
learning_goals: Efficiently navigate file systems using GUI tools (e.g., File Explorer, Finder).
Master essential CLI commands for file and directory management (e.g., cd, ls, mkdir).
Perform system configuration tasks using both GUI and CLI.
Understand when to use GUI vs. CLI for different operational needs.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: proxmox + Navigating using GUI and CLI Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz evaluates your understanding of Proxmox virtualization and your ability to navigate systems effectively using both GUI and CLI. It ensures you are proficient in managing diverse system environments.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on Proxmox installation, VM/container management, and features.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of GUI and CLI navigation and commands.
Understand when to apply different interface types for system interaction.
Achieve a passing score to advance in system administration and virtualization.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: default gateway
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson explains the concept of a default gateway, which is the router that serves as an access point to other networks, typically the internet. Understanding it is crucial for network connectivity and troubleshooting.
learning_goals: Define what a default gateway is and its role in network communication.
Explain how devices use the default gateway to reach external networks.
Identify the default gateway address on various operating systems.
Troubleshoot connectivity issues related to an incorrect default gateway.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Understand Permissions
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module covers the principles of file system and user permissions across different operating systems. Proper permission management is a fundamental security control to restrict unauthorized access to resources.
learning_goals: Understand different types of permissions (read, write, execute) in Windows and Linux.
Configure file and folder permissions using both GUI and CLI.
Explain the concept of user and group ownership for files and directories.
Implement the principle of least privilege through effective permission management.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: default gateway + Understand Permissions Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz tests your knowledge of network default gateways and the critical aspect of understanding and managing system permissions. It ensures you grasp key controls for network communication and resource access.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on the default gateway's function and troubleshooting.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of file and user permissions across OS platforms.
Apply the principle of least privilege in permission management scenarios.
Achieve a passing score to proceed with advanced system security configurations.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Hypervisor
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson provides a detailed explanation of hypervisors (Type 1 and Type 2), the software layer that creates and runs virtual machines. Understanding hypervisors is core to virtualization technology and cloud computing.
learning_goals: Define what a hypervisor is and its role in virtualization.
Differentiate between Type 1 (bare-metal) and Type 2 (hosted) hypervisors.
Understand the benefits and use cases for each hypervisor type.
Identify common hypervisor products (e.g., ESXi, Hyper-V, VirtualBox).

roadmap_checkpoints.label: GuestOS
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson focuses on the Guest OS, which is the operating system installed and running within a virtual machine. Understanding Guest OS interaction with the hypervisor is vital for VM management and security.
learning_goals: Define 'Guest OS' and its relationship to the Host OS and hypervisor.
Understand how a Guest OS interacts with virtualized hardware.
Identify challenges and best practices for managing Guest OS performance and security.
Configure Guest OS settings for optimal operation within a VM.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Installing Software and Applications
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module covers the process of installing, configuring, and managing software applications across various operating systems. Secure installation practices are essential to prevent vulnerabilities.
learning_goals: Perform software installation using package managers (e.g., apt, yum) and graphical installers.
Configure application settings and dependencies.
Understand the importance of verifying software integrity before installation.
Troubleshoot common software installation and compatibility issues.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: HostOS
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson explains the Host OS, the operating system directly installed on the physical hardware that runs the hypervisor and hosts virtual machines. Understanding its role is critical for managing virtualized environments.
learning_goals: Define 'Host OS' and its function in a virtualized environment.
Understand the hardware resources managed by the Host OS for VMs.
Identify the responsibilities of a Host OS administrator.
Implement security hardening measures for the Host OS.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Security Engineer Project #4
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This project requires you to build a multi-tiered application environment using virtual machines, implementing secure configurations for the Host OS, Hypervisor, and Guest OSes. You will also manage user permissions and software deployments.
learning_goals: Design and implement a multi-VM architecture with secure configurations.
Apply security hardening techniques to both Host and Guest Operating Systems.
Configure fine-grained user and file permissions across the environment.
Successfully deploy and secure an application stack within the virtualized infrastructure.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: VLAN
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson introduces Virtual Local Area Networks (VLANs), which logically segment a network without requiring separate physical hardware. VLANs are crucial for network organization, security, and performance.
learning_goals: Explain the concept and benefits of VLANs for network segmentation.
Differentiate between various VLAN types (e.g., access, trunk).
Configure VLANs on network switches.
Understand how VLANs enhance network security and reduce broadcast domains.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: DMZ
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module covers the Demilitarized Zone (DMZ), a physical or logical subnetwork that contains and exposes an organization's external-facing services to an untrusted network, usually the internet. It's a key component of network security architecture.
learning_goals: Define a DMZ and its strategic purpose in network security.
Understand how a DMZ provides a layer of security for internal networks.
Identify common services deployed within a DMZ (e.g., web servers, mail servers).
Design a basic network architecture incorporating a DMZ.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: ARP
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson explains the Address Resolution Protocol (ARP), a fundamental protocol used to map IP network addresses to hardware (MAC) addresses. Understanding ARP is essential for network communication and for detecting ARP spoofing attacks.
learning_goals: Explain the function of ARP in translating IP to MAC addresses.
Describe the ARP request and reply process.
Identify the contents of an ARP table.
Recognize the security implications of ARP, such as ARP spoofing.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: VLAN + DMZ + ARP Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz evaluates your understanding of VLANs for network segmentation, the strategic role of a DMZ in network security, and the Address Resolution Protocol (ARP). It ensures a strong grasp of foundational network architecture and security concepts.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on VLAN configuration and benefits.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of DMZ design and security considerations.
Explain the ARP protocol's function and identify ARP-related vulnerabilities.
Achieve a passing score to proceed with advanced network security topics.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: VM
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson provides a comprehensive understanding of virtual machines (VMs), their architecture, and lifecycle. It reinforces the importance of VMs in modern IT infrastructure, development, and security testing.
learning_goals: Define what a virtual machine is and its core components.
Explain the benefits of using VMs (e.g., isolation, portability).
Understand the lifecycle of a VM (creation, snapshot, suspension, deletion).
Identify use cases for VMs in development, testing, and security labs.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Performing CRUD on Files
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module covers the fundamental operations of Create, Read, Update, and Delete (CRUD) on files and directories. Mastering these operations is essential for file management, scripting, and system administration tasks.
learning_goals: Perform 'Create' operations to make new files and directories.
Execute 'Read' operations to view file contents and directory listings.
Apply 'Update' operations to modify existing file content.
Conduct 'Delete' operations to remove files and directories safely.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: VM + Performing CRUD on Files Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz tests your knowledge of virtual machine concepts and your proficiency in performing CRUD operations on files. It ensures you can effectively manage virtualized environments and manipulate data within them.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on virtual machine architecture and management.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of file creation, reading, updating, and deletion.
Apply CRUD operations efficiently using both GUI and command-line interfaces.
Achieve a passing score to advance in system and data management skills.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: NAT
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson explains Network Address Translation (NAT), a method of remapping one IP address space into another, typically for private network devices to access the internet. Understanding NAT is crucial for network design and security.
learning_goals: Explain the purpose and function of NAT in network communication.
Differentiate between different types of NAT (e.g., Static, Dynamic, PAT).
Understand how NAT conserves public IP addresses.
Identify the security implications and limitations of NAT.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: DHCP
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module covers the Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol (DHCP), which automatically assigns IP addresses and other network configuration parameters to devices on a network. DHCP is vital for network management.
learning_goals: Explain the purpose and operation of DHCP.
Describe the DHCP DORA process (Discovery, Offer, Request, Acknowledge).
Configure a basic DHCP server and client.
Understand the security considerations and vulnerabilities associated with DHCP.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: IP
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson provides a deep dive into the Internet Protocol (IP), covering its addressing, routing, and header structure for both IPv4 and IPv6. IP is the core protocol for all internet communication.
learning_goals: Understand the fundamental role of IP in network communication.
Differentiate between IPv4 and IPv6 addressing schemes and their benefits.
Explain how IP addresses are used for host identification and routing.
Analyze IP packet headers to understand data transmission.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Troubleshooting
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module teaches comprehensive troubleshooting techniques specific to network and system issues. Developing robust troubleshooting skills is essential for maintaining system uptime and responding to incidents effectively.
learning_goals: Apply a systematic approach to diagnose network and system problems.
Utilize common diagnostic tools (e.g., ping, tracert, netstat) for troubleshooting.
Identify and resolve issues related to IP addressing, DNS, and connectivity.
Document troubleshooting steps and solutions for future reference.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Security Engineer Project #5
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This project requires you to diagnose and resolve a series of complex network connectivity and service issues within a simulated environment. You'll apply your knowledge of NAT, DHCP, IP, and general troubleshooting to restore functionality.
learning_goals: Successfully identify and troubleshoot issues related to NAT and DHCP configurations.
Diagnose and resolve IP addressing and routing problems.
Utilize a range of diagnostic tools to pinpoint root causes of network failures.
Document troubleshooting steps and provide effective solutions under pressure.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Router
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers routers, network devices that forward data packets between computer networks. Understanding router functionality is fundamental to network design, traffic management, and security.
learning_goals: Explain the primary function of a router in a network.
Differentiate between routing protocols (e.g., static, dynamic).
Configure basic router settings and interfaces.
Understand how routers use IP addresses to forward packets.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Switch
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module introduces network switches, devices that connect devices on a computer network by using packet switching to forward data to the destination device. Understanding switches is crucial for designing and securing local area networks.
learning_goals: Explain the primary function of a network switch in a LAN.
Differentiate between hubs, switches, and routers.
Understand the concept of MAC address tables and how switches use them.
Configure basic switch settings (e.g., port security, VLAN assignment).

roadmap_checkpoints.label: VPN
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson explains Virtual Private Networks (VPNs), which create a secure, encrypted connection over a public network, like the internet. VPNs are essential for secure remote access and protecting data in transit.
learning_goals: Define what a VPN is and its primary security benefits.
Differentiate between common VPN protocols (e.g., IPSec, SSL/TLS VPNs).
Understand how VPNs provide confidentiality and integrity for network traffic.
Configure and troubleshoot basic VPN client connections.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Router + Switch + VPN Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz evaluates your understanding of essential networking devices like routers and switches, and the critical security technology of Virtual Private Networks (VPNs). It confirms your grasp of core network infrastructure and secure communication.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on router and switch functionalities and configuration.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of VPN protocols, benefits, and implementation.
Understand the role of each device in network design and security.
Achieve a passing score to advance in network infrastructure and secure access.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: netstat
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers the 'netstat' command, a powerful command-line utility for displaying network connections, routing tables, and interface statistics. It's a key tool for network diagnostics and security analysis.
learning_goals: Utilize 'netstat' to view active network connections.
Identify listening ports and associated processes.
Analyze routing table entries using 'netstat'.
Interpret network statistics for troubleshooting and security assessments.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: dig
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module introduces the 'dig' (Domain Information Groper) command, a flexible tool for querying DNS name servers. It's essential for diagnosing DNS issues and gathering domain-related intelligence.
learning_goals: Use 'dig' to perform various DNS queries (A, MX, NS, SOA records).
Interpret DNS query results and identify authoritative name servers.
Diagnose DNS resolution problems using 'dig'.
Gather open-source intelligence on domains using DNS records.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: ipconfig
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson explains the 'ipconfig' (Windows) and 'ifconfig' (Linux/macOS) commands, used to display and configure network interface parameters. These are fundamental for checking network connectivity and settings.
learning_goals: Utilize 'ipconfig' or 'ifconfig' to view network adapter configurations.
Identify IP addresses, subnet masks, and default gateways.
Release and renew DHCP leases using command-line tools.
Troubleshoot basic network connectivity by checking interface settings.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: netstat + dig + ipconfig Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz evaluates your proficiency with essential network diagnostic commands: 'netstat', 'dig', and 'ipconfig/ifconfig'. It confirms your ability to inspect network connections, resolve DNS issues, and verify network configurations.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on interpreting 'netstat' output for network analysis.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of using 'dig' for various DNS queries.
Apply 'ipconfig/ifconfig' to troubleshoot and verify network interface settings.
Achieve a passing score to advance in network diagnostics and security monitoring.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: ping
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers the 'ping' command, a fundamental network utility used to test the reachability of a host on an Internet Protocol (IP) network. It's an indispensable tool for basic connectivity testing.
learning_goals: Utilize the 'ping' command to test network host reachability.
Interpret 'ping' results, including packet loss and latency.
Understand ICMP (Internet Control Message Protocol) as used by 'ping'.
Diagnose basic network connectivity issues using 'ping'.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Common Commands
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module focuses on common command-line interface (CLI) commands across different operating systems (Windows, Linux, macOS). Mastering these commands enhances efficiency in system administration and security tasks.
learning_goals: Execute common file and directory management commands (e.g., cp, mv, rm, mkdir).
Perform process management tasks (e.g., ps, kill).
Utilize commands for system information and monitoring (e.g., top, df).
Understand the syntax and options of frequently used CLI commands.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Security Engineer Project #6
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This project tasks you with using a combination of common command-line utilities and network diagnostic tools to troubleshoot and secure a given system. You'll identify network configuration issues, inspect active connections, and apply basic security fixes.
learning_goals: Effectively use 'ping', 'netstat', 'dig', and 'ipconfig' for system analysis.
Diagnose and resolve basic network and system configuration problems via CLI.
Implement simple security measures (e.g., disabling unnecessary services, modifying firewall rules).
Document the troubleshooting process and implemented security improvements.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: tcpdump
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson introduces 'tcpdump', a powerful command-line packet analyzer for capturing and filtering network traffic. It's a crucial tool for network troubleshooting, security analysis, and protocol development.
learning_goals: Utilize 'tcpdump' to capture network packets from an interface.
Apply filters to 'tcpdump' to target specific traffic (e.g., by IP, port, protocol).
Interpret captured packet data to understand network communication.
Identify suspicious network activity through packet analysis.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: nmap
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module covers 'nmap' (Network Mapper), a free and open-source utility for network discovery and security auditing. 'nmap' is essential for reconnaissance and vulnerability scanning in security assessments.
learning_goals: Perform host discovery and port scanning using 'nmap'.
Identify open ports, services, and operating systems on target hosts.
Utilize 'nmap' scripting engine (NSE) for advanced vulnerability checks.
Interpret 'nmap' scan results for security posture assessment.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: route
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson explains the 'route' command, used to view and manipulate the IP routing table. Understanding and configuring routes is fundamental for controlling network traffic flow and ensuring connectivity.
learning_goals: Utilize the 'route' command to display the IP routing table.
Understand how routing tables determine packet forwarding decisions.
Add, delete, or modify static routes.
Diagnose routing issues and ensure proper network traffic flow.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: MAN
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module teaches how to effectively use 'man' (manual) pages, which provide documentation for commands and utilities in Unix-like operating systems. Mastering 'man' pages is key to self-sufficient learning and troubleshooting on the command line.
learning_goals: Access and navigate 'man' pages for various commands.
Identify relevant sections within a 'man' page (e.g., SYNOPSIS, DESCRIPTION, OPTIONS).
Understand how to find specific information or examples within documentation.
Utilize 'man' pages to learn new commands and troubleshoot usage.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: tcpdump + nmap + route + MAN Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz evaluates your proficiency with advanced command-line network tools ('tcpdump', 'nmap', 'route') and your ability to use 'man' pages for self-learning. It ensures you can perform deep network analysis and manage routing effectively.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on capturing and filtering traffic with 'tcpdump'.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of 'nmap' scanning techniques and result interpretation.
Apply 'route' command to view and modify network routing tables.
Effectively use 'man' pages to understand command functionalities and options.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: LAN
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson defines Local Area Networks (LANs), which are computer networks that interconnect computers within a limited area, such as a residence, school, or office building. Understanding LANs is foundational for all network engineers.
learning_goals: Define the characteristics and scope of a LAN.
Identify common components of a LAN (e.g., switches, cables, endpoints).
Understand the benefits and limitations of LANs.
Differentiate between various LAN topologies.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: WAN
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module covers Wide Area Networks (WANs), which span a large geographical area and often connect multiple LANs. Understanding WANs is crucial for designing and securing large-scale distributed networks.
learning_goals: Define the characteristics and scope of a WAN.
Identify common technologies used to implement WANs (e.g., MPLS, VPNs, leased lines).
Understand the benefits and challenges of WAN connectivity.
Differentiate between WAN and LAN technologies and use cases.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: LAN + WAN Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your understanding of Local Area Networks (LANs) and Wide Area Networks (WANs). It confirms your ability to differentiate between these fundamental network types and comprehend their respective characteristics and components.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on the definitions and characteristics of LANs.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of WAN technologies and their applications.
Identify common components and topologies for both LANs and WANs.
Achieve a passing score to progress in advanced network architecture concepts.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: WLAN
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson introduces Wireless Local Area Networks (WLANs), networks that link two or more devices using wireless communication to form a LAN. Understanding WLANs is vital for managing modern, mobile-friendly networks securely.
learning_goals: Explain the concept of WLANs and their operating principles.
Identify common WLAN standards (e.g., 802.11a/b/g/n/ac/ax).
Understand the components of a WLAN (e.g., access points, wireless clients).
Recognize the security challenges inherent in wireless networks.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: iptables
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module covers 'iptables', the user-space utility program used to configure the Linux kernel firewall. Mastering 'iptables' is essential for implementing robust host-based network security on Linux systems.
learning_goals: Understand the architecture and rule processing of 'iptables'.
Configure basic 'iptables' rules for filtering incoming and outgoing traffic.
Implement rules for NAT and stateful packet inspection.
Manage and persist 'iptables' configurations on a Linux system.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Security Engineer Project #7
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This project requires you to configure a secure WLAN, implement robust firewall rules using 'iptables' on a Linux system, and analyze network traffic for anomalies. You'll demonstrate practical skills in wireless security and host-based firewalls.
learning_goals: Successfully configure a secure Wireless Local Area Network (WLAN) with strong encryption.
Implement and manage 'iptables' rules to control network traffic flow and enhance host security.
Utilize packet analysis tools to monitor and identify suspicious activity on the network.
Document the implemented security configurations and observed traffic patterns.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: nslookup
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers 'nslookup', a command-line utility for querying Domain Name System (DNS) servers to obtain domain name or IP address mapping. It's a key tool for diagnosing DNS resolution issues.
learning_goals: Use 'nslookup' to resolve domain names to IP addresses and vice-versa.
Query different types of DNS records (e.g., A, MX, NS).
Diagnose DNS resolution problems using 'nslookup'.
Understand how DNS operates in resolving hostnames.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: tracert
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module introduces 'tracert' (Windows) or 'traceroute' (Linux/macOS), a command-line utility that displays the path (route) a packet takes to reach a destination. It's invaluable for network path analysis and troubleshooting.
learning_goals: Utilize 'tracert' or 'traceroute' to map the network path to a destination.
Interpret the output to identify network hops and latency.
Diagnose network routing issues and bottlenecks.
Understand how 'tracert' uses ICMP or UDP to determine routes.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Packet Sniffers
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson introduces packet sniffers, tools used to capture and analyze data packets traversing a computer network. Understanding and using packet sniffers (like Wireshark or tcpdump) is fundamental for network troubleshooting, security monitoring, and protocol analysis.
learning_goals: Explain the purpose and functionality of packet sniffers.
Identify various packet sniffer tools and their use cases.
Understand how to capture and filter network traffic.
Interpret packet data to analyze network communication and detect anomalies.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: IPAM
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module covers IP Address Management (IPAM), which is the administration of DNS and DHCP alongside the tracking of IP address space. IPAM is crucial for maintaining efficient, reliable, and secure network infrastructure.
learning_goals: Understand the role and benefits of IPAM in network management.
Explain how IPAM integrates DNS and DHCP services.
Identify best practices for tracking and allocating IP addresses.
Recognize the security benefits of centralized IPAM solutions.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: nslookup + tracert + Packet Sniffers + IPAM Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your understanding of DNS lookup tools ('nslookup'), network path diagnostics ('tracert'), the functionality of packet sniffers, and IP Address Management (IPAM). It ensures you can effectively diagnose network issues and manage IP resources.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on using 'nslookup' for DNS troubleshooting.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of 'tracert' for network path analysis.
Explain the principles and applications of packet sniffers.
Understand the importance and processes of IPAM for network stability and security.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Networking Knowledge
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module consolidates and deepens your overall networking knowledge, covering advanced topics in network design, protocols, and troubleshooting. It provides a holistic view of network operations relevant to security.
learning_goals: Reinforce understanding of the OSI and TCP/IP models with advanced concepts.
Analyze complex network topologies and traffic flows.
Evaluate various network technologies and their suitability for different scenarios.
Apply comprehensive networking knowledge to solve multifaceted problems.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Port Scanners
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson introduces port scanners, tools used to probe a server or host for open ports. Port scanning is a fundamental step in reconnaissance for security assessments to identify active services and potential vulnerabilities.
learning_goals: Explain the purpose and ethical considerations of port scanning.
Identify common port scanning techniques (e.g., TCP SYN, TCP connect, UDP scans).
Utilize popular port scanning tools (e.g., Nmap) to discover open ports and services.
Interpret port scan results to assess target security posture.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Protocol Analyzers
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module covers protocol analyzers, sophisticated tools (like Wireshark) that capture and dissect network packets to reveal the details of communication protocols. They are indispensable for deep network analysis and incident response.
learning_goals: Understand the functionality and benefits of protocol analyzers.
Use a protocol analyzer to capture and filter network traffic.
Dissect various network protocols (e.g., HTTP, DNS, FTP) at a deep level.
Identify protocol anomalies or malicious activity through detailed analysis.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Networking Knowledge + Port Scanners + Protocol Analyzers Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz comprehensively evaluates your overall networking knowledge, proficiency with port scanners for reconnaissance, and expertise in using protocol analyzers for deep network inspection. It confirms your readiness for advanced network security roles.
learning_goals: Successfully answer complex questions across various networking domains.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of port scanning techniques and their ethical use.
Apply protocol analysis skills to diagnose network issues and detect threats.
Achieve a passing score to signify expert-level networking and analysis capabilities.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Star
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson describes the Star network topology, where all nodes are individually connected to a central hub, switch, or computer. Understanding this common topology is key to network design and troubleshooting.
learning_goals: Define the characteristics and components of a Star topology.
Identify the advantages (e.g., ease of fault isolation) and disadvantages.
Understand the central role of the hub/switch in a Star network.
Recognize real-world examples of Star topology implementation.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Ring
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module covers the Ring network topology, where devices are connected in a circular fashion, with each device connected to exactly two others. Understanding this topology is important for historical context and specific industrial applications.
learning_goals: Define the characteristics and components of a Ring topology.
Understand how data flows unidirectionally or bidirectionally around the ring.
Identify the advantages (e.g., equal access) and disadvantages (e.g., single point of failure).
Recognize common protocols used in Ring networks (e.g., Token Ring).

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Security Engineer Project #8
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This project challenges you to design and implement a small network leveraging different topologies (e.g., star, ring, mesh) in a virtualized environment. You will secure each segment, configure protocols, and demonstrate robust network connectivity.
learning_goals: Design and implement network segments using various topologies.
Configure routing and switching to ensure seamless communication between segments.
Apply security controls appropriate for each topology and network zone.
Document the network design, configurations, and security measures comprehensively.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Mesh
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson describes the Mesh network topology, where every device is connected directly to every other device, providing high redundancy and fault tolerance. It's crucial for understanding highly resilient network designs.
learning_goals: Define the characteristics and components of a Mesh topology.
Differentiate between full mesh and partial mesh configurations.
Identify the advantages (e.g., redundancy, robust communication) and disadvantages (e.g., cost, complexity).
Understand suitable use cases for Mesh networks.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Bus
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module covers the Bus network topology, where all devices are connected to a single central cable (the bus). While less common in modern LANs, understanding it provides historical context for network evolution.
learning_goals: Define the characteristics and components of a Bus topology.
Understand how data is broadcast across the shared bus.
Identify the advantages (e.g., simple, low cost) and disadvantages (e.g., collision, fault isolation).
Recognize historical applications of Bus networks.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Understand the OSI Model
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson provides a deep dive into the Open Systems Interconnection (OSI) model, a conceptual framework that standardizes the functions of a telecommunication or computing system into seven layers. Understanding the OSI model is fundamental for troubleshooting and designing secure networks.
learning_goals: Identify and describe the function of each of the seven layers of the OSI model.
Understand how data flows through the OSI layers during communication.
Map common network protocols to their respective OSI layers.
Utilize the OSI model as a framework for network troubleshooting and security analysis.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Common Protocols and their Uses
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module covers a range of common network protocols beyond basic HTTP/DNS, explaining their purpose, operation, and security considerations. A broad understanding of protocols is vital for network monitoring and defense.
learning_goals: Explain the function and typical use cases of various common network protocols (e.g., SMTP, POP3, IMAP, SNMP).
Understand how these protocols operate at different layers of the OSI model.
Identify potential security vulnerabilities associated with each protocol.
Recognize the importance of securing protocol communications.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Mesh + Bus + Understand the OSI Model + Common Protocols and their Uses Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz comprehensively evaluates your knowledge of network topologies (Mesh, Bus), the foundational OSI Model, and common network protocols and their uses. It ensures a robust understanding of network architecture and communication principles.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on the characteristics and use cases of Mesh and Bus topologies.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of all seven layers of the OSI Model and their functions.
Identify common network protocols, their operational details, and security considerations.
Achieve a passing score to signify a strong foundation in network theory and design.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Kerberos
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers Kerberos, a network authentication protocol that provides strong authentication for client/server applications by using secret-key cryptography. It is widely used in enterprise environments like Active Directory.
learning_goals: Explain the Kerberos authentication process and key components (KDC, client, server).
Understand the role of tickets and ticket-granting tickets (TGTs).
Identify the security benefits of Kerberos (e.g., mutual authentication, replay attack prevention).
Recognize common Kerberos attack vectors (e.g., Golden Ticket, Silver Ticket).

roadmap_checkpoints.label: LDAP
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module introduces LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol), an open, vendor-neutral, industry-standard application protocol for accessing and maintaining distributed directory information services. It's fundamental for centralized user management.
learning_goals: Explain the purpose and architecture of LDAP directory services.
Understand basic LDAP operations (e.g., bind, search, add, modify, delete).
Identify the structure of an LDAP directory (e.g., DN, RDN, attributes).
Recognize the security implications of LDAP (e.g., unencrypted traffic, injection attacks).

roadmap_checkpoints.label: SSO
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson explains Single Sign-On (SSO), an authentication scheme that allows a user to log in with a single ID and password to gain access to multiple related yet independent software systems. SSO enhances user experience and security.
learning_goals: Define SSO and its advantages for user convenience and security.
Understand the underlying principles of SSO protocols (e.g., SAML, OAuth, OpenID Connect).
Identify the key components involved in an SSO transaction.
Recognize the security considerations and potential vulnerabilities of SSO implementations.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Kerberos + LDAP + SSO Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your understanding of critical authentication and directory services: Kerberos, LDAP, and Single Sign-On (SSO). It ensures you can comprehend and secure centralized identity management systems.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on the Kerberos authentication process and components.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of LDAP directory structures and operations.
Explain the benefits and underlying mechanisms of Single Sign-On (SSO).
Identify security considerations and common attack vectors for each technology.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: RADIUS
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers RADIUS (Remote Authentication Dial-In User Service), a networking protocol that provides centralized Authentication, Authorization, and Accounting (AAA) management for users connecting to a network service. It's common in enterprise network access.
learning_goals: Explain the purpose and function of the RADIUS protocol.
Understand the components of a RADIUS system (client, server, backend DB).
Differentiate between authentication, authorization, and accounting in RADIUS.
Recognize the use cases for RADIUS, especially in network access control.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: RDP
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module introduces RDP (Remote Desktop Protocol), a proprietary protocol developed by Microsoft that allows a user to graphically control another computer remotely. Securing RDP is critical due to its common use and attack surface.
learning_goals: Understand the functionality and common use cases of RDP.
Explain how RDP facilitates remote graphical access to computers.
Identify security risks associated with RDP (e.g., brute-force attacks, credential theft).
Implement security best practices for RDP (e.g., strong passwords, MFA, network-level authentication).

roadmap_checkpoints.label: FTP
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson explains FTP (File Transfer Protocol), a standard network protocol used for the transfer of computer files between a client and server on a computer network. While widely used, its security limitations are important to understand.
learning_goals: Explain the basic operation of FTP for file transfer.
Differentiate between active and passive FTP modes.
Understand the security weaknesses of FTP (e.g., clear-text credentials).
Recognize when FTP is an appropriate or inappropriate protocol to use.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Security Engineer Project #9
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This project involves setting up and securing various authentication and remote access services, including RADIUS, RDP, and FTP/SFTP, in a controlled lab environment. You'll focus on implementing strong authentication mechanisms and hardening service configurations.
learning_goals: Successfully configure and test a RADIUS server for network access control.
Implement secure RDP access with strong authentication and network restrictions.
Set up an FTP server and demonstrate its security vulnerabilities.
Configure an SFTP server as a secure alternative and verify its encryption.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: SFTP
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson introduces SFTP (SSH File Transfer Protocol), a secure file transfer protocol that provides file access, file transfer, and file management functionalities over any reliable data stream. It addresses the security limitations of FTP.
learning_goals: Explain the purpose and security advantages of SFTP over FTP.
Understand how SFTP leverages SSH for secure communication.
Perform file transfer and management operations using SFTP clients.
Identify scenarios where SFTP is the preferred protocol for file transfer.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Common Ports and their Uses
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module provides an overview of common network ports and their associated services, emphasizing well-known ports. Understanding these mappings is crucial for network configuration, firewall rules, and security monitoring.
learning_goals: Identify well-known ports (0-1023) and their default services (e.g., 21, 22, 23, 25, 53, 80, 443).
Understand the concept of registered ports and dynamic/private ports.
Explain how port numbers are used by TCP/UDP to direct traffic to applications.
Recognize the security implications of open ports and unnecessary services.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: SFTP + Common Ports and their Uses Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your understanding of SFTP as a secure file transfer protocol and your knowledge of common network ports and their associated uses. It ensures you can secure file transfers and manage network access based on port information.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on the security benefits and operation of SFTP.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of well-known network ports and their services.
Identify appropriate ports for common network applications and protocols.
Achieve a passing score to progress in secure network service configuration.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Certificates
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers digital certificates, specifically X.509, which are electronic documents used to prove the ownership of a public key. Certificates are fundamental to public key infrastructure (PKI) and secure communication like SSL/TLS.
learning_goals: Explain the purpose and components of a digital certificate (e.g., public key, issuer, subject).
Understand the role of Certificate Authorities (CAs) in issuing and verifying certificates.
Differentiate between various types of certificates (e.g., SSL/TLS, code signing).
Recognize the importance of certificate validity and revocation for security.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Local Auth
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module focuses on local authentication mechanisms, where user credentials are verified against a local database or file on a specific system. Understanding local auth is important for standalone systems and as a fallback.
learning_goals: Define local authentication and its typical implementation.
Understand how local user accounts and passwords are managed.
Identify the security strengths and weaknesses of local authentication.
Implement secure password policies for local user accounts.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: SSL and TLS Basics
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson introduces the basics of SSL (Secure Sockets Layer) and TLS (Transport Layer Security), cryptographic protocols that provide secure communication over a computer network. They are essential for protecting data in transit on the internet.
learning_goals: Explain the purpose of SSL/TLS in providing confidentiality and integrity for web traffic.
Understand the basic handshake process between client and server.
Differentiate between SSL and TLS versions and their security implications.
Identify when and why SSL/TLS encryption is used (e.g., HTTPS, VPNs).

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Basics of NAS and SAN
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module covers the basics of Network Attached Storage (NAS) and Storage Area Network (SAN) technologies. Understanding these storage solutions is important for managing data infrastructure and ensuring data availability and security.
learning_goals: Differentiate between NAS and SAN storage architectures.
Understand the primary use cases and benefits of each storage type.
Identify common protocols used by NAS (e.g., NFS, SMB/CIFS) and SAN (e.g., Fibre Channel, iSCSI).
Recognize the security considerations for securing NAS and SAN environments.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Certificates + Local Auth + SSL and TLS Basics + Basics of NAS and SAN Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your understanding of digital certificates, local authentication mechanisms, the basics of SSL/TLS, and the concepts of NAS and SAN storage. It ensures you can secure data at rest and in transit, and manage local access controls.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on digital certificates and PKI principles.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of local authentication processes and best practices.
Explain the fundamental concepts of SSL/TLS encryption and its handshake.
Understand the differences and security considerations for NAS and SAN storage solutions.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Security Skills and Knowledge
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module provides a comprehensive overview of fundamental security skills and knowledge essential for a security engineer. It integrates concepts across various domains to build a holistic security mindset.
learning_goals: Articulate the importance of security principles in system design and operation.
Identify common security threats, vulnerabilities, and attacks.
Understand the core security concepts of confidentiality, integrity, and availability.
Apply risk management principles to security decision-making.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Core Concepts of Zero Trust
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson introduces the Core Concepts of Zero Trust, a security model based on the principle of 'never trust, always verify'. It requires strict identity verification for every person and device trying to access resources, regardless of their location.
learning_goals: Explain the fundamental principles of the Zero Trust security model.
Understand why Zero Trust has become a critical approach in modern cybersecurity.
Identify key components of a Zero Trust architecture (e.g., MFA, micro-segmentation, least privilege).
Differentiate Zero Trust from traditional perimeter-based security.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Security Engineer Project #10
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This project challenges you to design and implement a Zero Trust access policy for a critical application within a simulated corporate network. You will configure identity verification, micro-segmentation, and granular access controls.
learning_goals: Apply Zero Trust principles to design secure access policies for an application.
Implement multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all access attempts.
Configure network micro-segmentation to isolate the application.
Document the Zero Trust architecture, policies, and their enforcement mechanisms.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Understand Common Hacking Tools
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module covers common hacking tools used by both ethical hackers and malicious actors for reconnaissance, scanning, exploitation, and post-exploitation. Understanding these tools is crucial for both offense and defense.
learning_goals: Identify and describe the purpose of common hacking tools (e.g., Nmap, Metasploit, Wireshark, Burp Suite).
Understand how these tools are used in different phases of a cyberattack.
Recognize the capabilities and limitations of various hacking tools.
Explain how security professionals use these tools for vulnerability assessment and penetration testing.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Roles of Compliance and Auditors
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson explores the critical Roles of Compliance and Auditors in maintaining an organization's security posture and meeting regulatory requirements. Understanding these roles is vital for aligning security efforts with business and legal obligations.
learning_goals: Understand the purpose and importance of regulatory compliance (e.g., GDPR, HIPAA, PCI DSS).
Explain the role of security auditors in assessing compliance and identifying gaps.
Differentiate between internal and external audits.
Recognize how security engineers contribute to and support compliance efforts.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Understand Common Hacking Tools + Roles of Compliance and Auditors Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz evaluates your understanding of common hacking tools and the crucial roles of compliance and auditors in cybersecurity. It ensures you grasp both offensive techniques and the regulatory framework governing security practices.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on the functionality and ethical use of common hacking tools.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of compliance standards and auditing processes.
Identify how security engineering practices align with regulatory requirements.
Achieve a passing score to progress in ethical hacking and governance, risk, and compliance (GRC).

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Blue / Red / Purple Teams
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson introduces the concepts of Blue Teams, Red Teams, and Purple Teams within cybersecurity. Understanding these roles is vital for appreciating different defensive and offensive strategies and how they integrate.
learning_goals: Differentiate between the roles and objectives of Blue Teams (defense) and Red Teams (offense).
Understand the purpose and benefits of Purple Teams in improving security posture.
Explain how these teams collaborate to enhance organizational security.
Identify the skills and responsibilities typically associated with each team type.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Understand Common Exploit Frameworks
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module covers common exploit frameworks, such as Metasploit, which provide a structured approach to developing, testing, and executing exploits. Understanding these frameworks is key for both offensive and defensive security.
learning_goals: Explain the purpose and components of common exploit frameworks.
Understand how exploits are developed and deployed using these frameworks.
Utilize an exploit framework (e.g., Metasploit) to demonstrate vulnerabilities in a controlled environment.
Recognize how defensive measures can detect and mitigate attacks launched from these frameworks.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Understand the Definition of Risk
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson clarifies the fundamental Definition of Risk in cybersecurity, encompassing assets, threats, vulnerabilities, and their potential impact. A clear understanding of risk is essential for effective security management.
learning_goals: Define cybersecurity risk as a combination of threat, vulnerability, and impact.
Identify critical assets within an organization and their value.
Distinguish between threats (potential harm) and vulnerabilities (weaknesses).
Calculate and prioritize risks based on likelihood and impact.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: False Negative / False Positive
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module explains the concepts of False Negatives and False Positives in security detection systems. Understanding these concepts is crucial for evaluating the effectiveness of security controls and avoiding alert fatigue.
learning_goals: Define a False Negative in the context of security detection (e.g., an attack missed).
