ACLs are fundamental to access control.
learning_goals: Explain the purpose and structure of Access Control Lists.
Differentiate between file system ACLs and network ACLs.
Configure basic ACLs to control access to files, directories, and network resources.
Understand how ACLs enforce granular permissions based on user or group identities.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Sandboxing + ACLs Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your understanding of sandboxing for secure execution and Access Control Lists (ACLs) for granular permissions. It ensures you can isolate untrusted code and enforce precise access controls on systems and networks.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on sandboxing principles and its security benefits.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of ACLs for file system and network access control.
Apply ACLs to implement the principle of least privilege.
Achieve a passing score to progress in secure execution and access management.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Patching
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers Patching, the process of applying software updates and fixes to address bugs and security vulnerabilities. Effective patch management is a critical security practice to reduce an organization's attack surface.
learning_goals: Explain the importance of regular patching for security.
Understand the lifecycle of patch management (e.g., discovery, testing, deployment, verification).
Identify different types of patches (e.g., security, bug fixes, feature enhancements).
Recognize the risks associated with unpatched systems and applications.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Deauth Attack
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module introduces the Deauthentication (Deauth) Attack, a type of denial-of-service attack on a Wi-Fi network that disconnects authorized users from a wireless access point. Understanding this attack is crucial for wireless security.
learning_goals: Define a deauthentication attack and explain its mechanism.
Understand how attackers can forcibly disconnect users from a Wi-Fi network.
Identify the tools and techniques used to perform deauth attacks.
Implement defensive measures against deauth attacks (e.g., strong authentication, monitoring).

roadmap_checkpoints.label: ACL
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson focuses on Access Control List (ACL) as a singular concept, reinforcing its role as a fundamental security mechanism. It covers the general principles of defining permissions to resources.
learning_goals: Define what an Access Control List (ACL) is at a conceptual level.
Understand how ACLs are used to permit or deny access to resources.
Identify the basic elements typically found in an ACL entry.
Explain the order of processing for ACL rules.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: EAP vs PEAP
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module differentiates between EAP (Extensible Authentication Protocol) and PEAP (Protected Extensible Authentication Protocol), common authentication protocols used in wireless and wired networks. Understanding them is crucial for secure network access.
learning_goals: Explain the purpose of EAP as a framework for authentication.
Understand how PEAP builds on EAP to provide a secure, tunneled authentication method.
Differentiate between EAP and PEAP in terms of security and implementation.
Recognize the use cases for EAP and PEAP in secure network access (e.g., 802.1X, WPA2-Enterprise).

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Patching + Deauth Attack + ACL + EAP vs PEAP Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your understanding of critical security practices (patching), wireless attack vectors (Deauth Attack), foundational access control (ACL), and advanced authentication protocols (EAP vs. PEAP). It ensures you can secure systems, wireless networks, and access controls.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on patch management best practices and importance.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of deauthentication attacks and their mitigation.
Apply ACL concepts to define resource permissions effectively.
Differentiate between EAP and PEAP and their role in secure network authentication.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: WPS
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers WPS (Wi-Fi Protected Setup), a network security standard that attempts to make the process of securing a wireless home network easier for users. However, WPS has known vulnerabilities that attackers can exploit.
learning_goals: Explain the purpose and operation of WPS.
Identify the security vulnerabilities associated with WPS (e.g., PIN brute-force).
Understand how attackers can exploit WPS to gain access to a Wi-Fi network.
Recognize the importance of disabling WPS for enhanced wireless security.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Endpoint Security
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module introduces Endpoint Security, a comprehensive approach to protecting end-user devices like laptops, desktops, and mobile phones from cyber threats. It encompasses various security layers beyond just antivirus.
learning_goals: Define endpoint security and its broad scope.
Identify key components of a robust endpoint security solution (e.g., AV, EDR, host firewall, DLP).
Understand the importance of securing endpoints as a primary attack vector.
Explain how endpoint security contributes to an organization's overall security posture.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Replay Attack
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers the Replay Attack, a type of network attack where a valid data transmission is maliciously or fraudulently repeated. Understanding replay attacks is crucial for designing secure communication protocols.
learning_goals: Define a replay attack and its mechanism.
Understand how attackers can capture and retransmit legitimate data.
Identify common scenarios where replay attacks are possible.
Implement defensive measures against replay attacks (e.g., timestamps, nonces, session tokens).

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Security Engineer Project #22
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This project tasks you with implementing and testing a comprehensive endpoint security solution, including defending against wireless vulnerabilities like WPS and replay attacks. You'll demonstrate proactive endpoint protection and wireless security hardening.
learning_goals: Deploy and configure a robust endpoint security suite on a target workstation.
Identify and mitigate WPS vulnerabilities on a wireless access point.
Simulate and detect a replay attack on a network, then implement countermeasures.
Document the endpoint security configuration and the steps taken to harden wireless security.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: WPA vs WPA2 vs WPA3 vs WEP
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson provides a comprehensive comparison of various Wi-Fi security protocols: WEP, WPA, WPA2, and WPA3. Understanding their evolution, strengths, and weaknesses is crucial for securing wireless networks.
learning_goals: Explain the security flaws of WEP and why it is deprecated.
Differentiate between WPA and WPA2, highlighting the improvements of WPA2.
Understand the key security enhancements of WPA3, including SAE and Opportunistic Wireless Encryption.
Choose the appropriate Wi-Fi security protocol based on security requirements and compatibility.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Rogue Access Point
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module covers Rogue Access Points, unauthorized wireless access points installed on a network. Rogue APs pose a significant security risk as they can provide attackers with unauthorized access to the network or facilitate Man-in-the-Middle attacks.
learning_goals: Define a rogue access point and explain its security implications.
Understand how rogue APs can be installed and detected.
Identify the risks associated with rogue APs (e.g., unauthorized access, data interception).
Implement strategies to detect and mitigate rogue access points on a network.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: any.run
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson introduces 'any.run', an interactive malware sandbox that allows users to analyze the behavior of suspicious files and URLs in a safe, controlled environment. It's an essential tool for malware analysis and threat intelligence.
learning_goals: Understand the capabilities and benefits of interactive malware sandboxing.
Utilize 'any.run' to submit and analyze suspicious files or URLs.
Interpret the behavioral analysis reports (e.g., network connections, file modifications).
Identify malicious indicators and draw conclusions about malware functionality.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: WPA vs WPA2 vs WPA3 vs WEP + Rogue Access Point + any.run Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your understanding of Wi-Fi security protocols (WEP, WPA, WPA2, WPA3), the threat of Rogue Access Points, and your ability to use 'any.run' for malware analysis. It ensures you can secure wireless networks and analyze suspicious executables.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on the security features and weaknesses of WEP, WPA, WPA2, and WPA3.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of rogue access points and their detection/mitigation.
Apply 'any.run' to perform interactive malware analysis and interpret results.
Achieve a passing score to progress in wireless security and malware forensics.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: urlscan
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson introduces 'urlscan.io', a free service that analyzes URLs and shows you the components of a website, including requests, domains, IP addresses, and a screenshot. It's useful for website security analysis and phishing detection.
learning_goals: Understand the purpose and capabilities of 'urlscan.io' for website analysis.
Utilize 'urlscan.io' to analyze suspicious URLs and retrieve website information.
Interpret scan results to identify malicious indicators (e.g., redirects, hidden iframes).
Recognize how 'urlscan.io' aids in phishing detection and threat intelligence.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Buffer Overflow
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module covers Buffer Overflow, a common software vulnerability where a program attempts to write data to a buffer that is larger than the buffer's allocated size. This can lead to crashes, arbitrary code execution, and privilege escalation.
learning_goals: Define buffer overflow and explain its underlying mechanism.
Understand how buffer overflows can lead to memory corruption and control flow hijacking.
Identify common types of buffer overflows (e.g., stack-based, heap-based).
Recognize mitigation techniques to prevent buffer overflows (e.g., ASLR, DEP, bounds checking).

roadmap_checkpoints.label: urlscan + Buffer Overflow Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your understanding of 'urlscan.io' for website security analysis and the critical software vulnerability of Buffer Overflow. It ensures you can investigate suspicious URLs and comprehend fundamental code exploitation techniques.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on using 'urlscan.io' to analyze website components and threats.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of buffer overflow vulnerabilities and their exploitation.
Explain common mitigation strategies for buffer overflows.
Achieve a passing score to progress in web security analysis and exploit development.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Preparation
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers the Preparation phase of the Incident Response lifecycle. This phase focuses on activities undertaken before an incident occurs to ensure an organization is ready to handle potential security breaches effectively.
learning_goals: Understand the key activities involved in the preparation phase (e.g., policy development, training).
Identify essential resources and tools needed for effective incident response.
Explain the importance of an incident response team (IRT) and its structure.
Develop basic incident response plans and playbooks.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Identification
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module introduces the Identification phase of the Incident Response lifecycle, where security incidents are detected, analyzed, and confirmed. This phase is crucial for early detection and understanding the scope of a breach.
learning_goals: Understand the primary goals of the identification phase (e.g., detection, validation, analysis).
Identify common indicators of compromise (IoCs) and attack methods.
Explain the process of collecting and analyzing evidence during identification.
Determine the scope and severity of a detected security incident.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Containment
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers the Containment phase of the Incident Response lifecycle, which aims to limit the scope and impact of a security incident. Effective containment minimizes damage and prevents further spread of an attack.
learning_goals: Understand the primary goals of the containment phase (e.g., isolate, stop spread).
Identify various containment strategies (e.g., network segmentation, system shutdown).
Explain the importance of balancing containment with evidence preservation.
Develop short-term and long-term containment plans.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: urlvoid
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module introduces 'urlvoid.com', a free service that aggregates multiple online scanning engines and website reputation tools to check a URL for malware, phishing, and other threats. It's a quick way to assess a website's safety reputation.
learning_goals: Understand the purpose and capabilities of 'urlvoid.com' for URL reputation checking.
Utilize 'urlvoid.com' to analyze suspicious URLs for malware and phishing indicators.
Interpret the aggregated scan results from various engines.
Recognize how 'urlvoid.com' aids in rapid threat assessment.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Security Engineer Project #23
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This project tasks you with developing an incident response plan covering the 'Preparation', 'Identification', and 'Containment' phases for a simulated cyberattack. You will integrate URL analysis tools like 'urlscan' and 'urlvoid' into your identification process.
learning_goals: Develop a comprehensive incident response plan for the initial stages of a cyberattack.
Define and document procedures for the 'Preparation' and 'Identification' phases.
Integrate URL analysis tools ('urlscan', 'urlvoid') into the identification workflow for suspicious links.
Formulate effective 'Containment' strategies to limit the impact of the attack.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: WHOIS
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson introduces 'WHOIS', a query and response protocol widely used for querying databases that store the registered users or assignees of an Internet resource, such as a domain name or an IP address. It's a key tool for reconnaissance and incident response.
learning_goals: Explain the purpose and functionality of the WHOIS protocol.
Utilize WHOIS lookups to gather information about domain names and IP addresses.
Interpret WHOIS records to identify domain registrants, registrars, and registration dates.
Recognize how WHOIS data can be used in reconnaissance and incident investigation.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Memory Leak
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module covers Memory Leaks, a type of resource leak that occurs when a computer program incorrectly manages memory allocations, resulting in a gradual decrease in available memory. Memory leaks can lead to system instability, performance degradation, and denial-of-service conditions.
learning_goals: Define memory leak and explain its impact on system performance and stability.
Understand the common programming errors that lead to memory leaks.
Identify tools and techniques for detecting and diagnosing memory leaks.
Recognize the security implications of memory leaks (e.g., DoS, information disclosure).

roadmap_checkpoints.label: WHOIS + Memory Leak Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your understanding of 'WHOIS' for domain and IP information gathering and the critical software vulnerability of Memory Leaks. It ensures you can perform effective reconnaissance and identify code quality issues with security implications.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on utilizing WHOIS for intelligence gathering and investigation.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of memory leak causes, impacts, and detection.
Identify the security risks associated with memory leaks.
Achieve a passing score to progress in reconnaissance and secure coding practices.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Eradication
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers the Eradication phase of the Incident Response lifecycle, which focuses on removing the root cause of an incident, such as deleting malware, patching vulnerabilities, or improving configurations. Effective eradication prevents re-infection.
learning_goals: Understand the primary goals of the eradication phase (e.g., eliminate root cause).
Identify common eradication activities (e.g., malware removal, system re-imaging, patch deployment).
Explain the importance of confirming the complete removal of the threat.
Develop strategies to prevent re-infection after eradication.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Recovery
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module introduces the Recovery phase of the Incident Response lifecycle, which involves restoring affected systems and services to normal operation. This phase ensures business continuity after an incident.
learning_goals: Understand the primary goals of the recovery phase (e.g., restore services, validate functionality).
Identify key recovery activities (e.g., restoring from backups, system hardening, monitoring).
Explain the importance of validating system integrity and functionality post-incident.
Develop strategies for phased recovery and long-term stabilization.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Eradication + Recovery Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your understanding of the 'Eradication' and 'Recovery' phases of the Incident Response lifecycle. It ensures you can effectively remove threats from systems and restore operations to a secure state after a cyberattack.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on the objectives and activities of the eradication phase.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of the recovery phase, including data restoration and system hardening.
Identify the logical sequence and dependencies between eradication and recovery.
Achieve a passing score to progress in incident response and business continuity.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Lessons Learned
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers the 'Lessons Learned' phase of the Incident Response lifecycle, where the incident is reviewed to identify areas for improvement in policies, procedures, and security controls. This crucial step drives continuous security enhancement.
learning_goals: Understand the importance of the 'Lessons Learned' phase for continuous improvement.
Identify key activities in a post-incident review (e.g., what went well, what went wrong).
Explain how to translate lessons learned into actionable recommendations.
Develop a process for implementing improvements based on incident analysis.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Pass the Hash
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module introduces 'Pass the Hash' (PtH), an attack technique where an attacker authenticates to a remote server or service by using a password hash instead of the corresponding plaintext password. PtH exploits weak authentication protocols.
learning_goals: Define Pass the Hash (PtH) and explain its underlying mechanism.
Understand how attackers can acquire password hashes and use them for authentication.
Identify operating systems and protocols vulnerable to PtH attacks.
Implement defensive measures against PtH attacks (e.g., LAPS, credential guarding, network segmentation).

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Legal
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers the Legal aspects relevant to cybersecurity, including privacy laws, data breach notification requirements, intellectual property, and ethical considerations for security professionals. Understanding these legal frameworks is crucial for compliance and risk management.
learning_goals: Identify key cybersecurity laws and regulations (e.g., GDPR, CCPA, HIPAA).
Understand data breach notification requirements and implications.
Explain ethical guidelines for security professionals (e.g., legal authorization, scope).
Recognize the legal ramifications of cybercrimes and security incidents.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Zero Day
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module explains Zero-Day vulnerabilities, which are unknown software flaws that have not yet been discovered by the vendor or for which no patch is available. Zero-day exploits pose significant threats due to their novelty and lack of immediate defense.
learning_goals: Define a zero-day vulnerability and its characteristics.
Understand the severe impact of zero-day exploits on security.
Identify how zero-day vulnerabilities are discovered and exploited by attackers.
Implement defensive strategies to mitigate the risks of zero-day attacks (e.g., defense in depth, behavioral analysis).

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Security Engineer Project #24
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This project tasks you with performing a comprehensive post-incident analysis ('Lessons Learned') of a simulated 'Pass the Hash' attack scenario. You will identify vulnerabilities, propose remediation, and incorporate legal and zero-day considerations into your enhanced incident response plan.
learning_goals: Conduct a thorough 'Lessons Learned' review of a simulated Pass the Hash attack.
Identify root causes, security control failures, and process gaps from the attack.
Propose technical and procedural remediation to prevent future PtH attacks, considering legal implications.
Develop an enhanced incident response plan that accounts for zero-day vulnerabilities and legal compliance.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Known vs Unknown
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson differentiates between Known and Unknown vulnerabilities and threats. Understanding this distinction is critical for prioritizing security efforts and developing strategies to mitigate both identified and undiscovered risks.
learning_goals: Define known vulnerabilities (e.g., CVEs) and threats (e.g., common malware).
Define unknown vulnerabilities (e.g., zero-days) and threats (e.g., APTs).
Understand the challenges of defending against unknown threats.
Explain strategies for managing risks associated with both known and unknown threats.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: APT
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module covers Advanced Persistent Threats (APTs), which are sophisticated, long-term cyberattacks typically carried out by nation-states or highly organized criminal groups. Understanding APTs is crucial for high-security environments.
learning_goals: Define an Advanced Persistent Threat (APT) and its key characteristics (e.g., stealth, persistence, specific targeting).
Understand the typical stages and tools used by APT groups.
Identify the motivations and targets of APTs.
Implement advanced defensive strategies to detect and respond to APTs.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Management
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson introduces Security Management principles, which encompass the overall governance, policies, and processes for protecting information assets. Effective security management aligns security with business objectives and manages risk.
learning_goals: Explain the role of security management in an organization.
Understand the key components of an Information Security Management System (ISMS).
Identify best practices for security policy development and enforcement.
Recognize the importance of continuous monitoring and improvement in security management.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Known vs Unknown + APT + Management Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your understanding of Known vs. Unknown threats, Advanced Persistent Threats (APTs), and Security Management principles. It ensures you can differentiate threat types, recognize sophisticated adversaries, and apply overarching security governance.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on the characteristics and mitigation of known vs. unknown threats.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of APT tactics, techniques, and procedures.
Explain key principles of security management and governance.
Achieve a passing score to progress in strategic cybersecurity planning and threat intelligence.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Directory Traversal
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers Directory Traversal (also known as path traversal), a web security vulnerability that allows an attacker to access files and directories stored outside the web root folder. Understanding it is critical for web application security.
learning_goals: Define directory traversal and explain its underlying mechanism.
Understand how an attacker can manipulate file paths to access unauthorized files.
Identify common input sanitization failures that lead to directory traversal.
Implement defensive measures to prevent directory traversal vulnerabilities (e.g., input validation, canonicalization).

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Cloud Skills and Knowledge
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module provides a comprehensive overview of Cloud Skills and Knowledge essential for a security engineer, covering concepts relevant to securing cloud environments. It integrates security principles into cloud computing contexts.
learning_goals: Understand the shared responsibility model in cloud security.
Identify common cloud security challenges and best practices.
Explain the security implications of various cloud service models (IaaS, PaaS, SaaS).
Recognize key cloud security technologies and controls.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Directory Traversal + Cloud Skills and Knowledge Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your understanding of Directory Traversal vulnerabilities in web applications and your foundational Cloud Skills and Knowledge. It ensures you can secure web services and understand the unique security aspects of cloud environments.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on identifying and mitigating directory traversal attacks.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of cloud security concepts and the shared responsibility model.
Identify common cloud security challenges and solutions.
Achieve a passing score to progress in cloud security and web application hardening.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Understand the Concept of Security in the Cloud
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers the fundamental Concept of Security in the Cloud, emphasizing the shared responsibility model between cloud providers and customers. Understanding this model is critical for effective cloud security strategy.
learning_goals: Explain the shared responsibility model in cloud computing (customer vs. provider responsibilities).
Understand how security is implemented at different layers of the cloud stack.
Identify common cloud security best practices and challenges.
Recognize the importance of configuring cloud services securely.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: SaaS
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module introduces Software as a Service (SaaS), a cloud service model where applications are hosted by a vendor and made available to customers over the internet. Understanding SaaS security is crucial as it's a widely adopted model.
learning_goals: Define SaaS and identify its key characteristics.
Understand the security considerations unique to the SaaS model.
Explain customer responsibilities for security in a SaaS environment.
Evaluate the security features and compliance of common SaaS applications.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Understand the differences between cloud and on-premises
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson explains the critical differences between cloud and on-premises deployment models, focusing on architecture, management, scalability, and security implications. This comparison is essential for making informed infrastructure decisions.
learning_goals: Differentiate between cloud and on-premises infrastructure deployments.
Understand the operational and cost differences between the two models.
Identify the distinct security considerations and challenges for each environment.
Explain how the shared responsibility model applies to cloud vs. on-premises.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Understand the concept of Infrastructure as Code
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module introduces the concept of Infrastructure as Code (IaC), the managing and provisioning of infrastructure through code rather than through manual processes. IaC is crucial for automating cloud deployments securely and consistently.
learning_goals: Define Infrastructure as Code (IaC) and its benefits for cloud management.
Understand how IaC enables consistent, repeatable, and auditable infrastructure deployments.
Identify popular IaC tools (e.g., Terraform, CloudFormation, Ansible).
Explain how IaC contributes to security by reducing manual errors and enabling version control.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Security Engineer Project #25
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This project tasks you with designing and implementing a secure cloud environment for a web application, differentiating between cloud and on-premises security considerations. You will use Infrastructure as Code (IaC) to define and deploy secure cloud resources.
learning_goals: Design a secure cloud architecture for a web application, incorporating best practices.
Differentiate and apply security controls relevant to cloud vs. on-premises deployments.
Utilize Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools to provision and configure cloud resources securely.
Demonstrate the benefits of IaC for consistency and security in cloud deployments.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Private
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson introduces Private Cloud, a cloud computing model where computing resources are dedicated to a single organization. Understanding private cloud offers insights into hybrid deployments and compliance-driven environments.
learning_goals: Define private cloud and its key characteristics (e.g., dedicated resources, control).
Understand the benefits of private cloud for security, compliance, and control.
Differentiate private cloud from public and hybrid cloud models.
Identify use cases and deployment considerations for private cloud environments.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Understand the Concept of Serverless
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module covers the Concept of Serverless computing (often Function as a Service, FaaS), a cloud execution model where the cloud provider dynamically manages the allocation and provisioning of servers. Understanding serverless security is crucial for modern cloud development.
learning_goals: Define serverless computing and its key characteristics (e.g., event-driven, ephemeral).
Understand the security implications and challenges unique to serverless architectures.
Identify common serverless platforms (e.g., AWS Lambda, Azure Functions).
Implement security best practices for serverless functions and applications.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Understand the basics and general flow of deploying in the cloud
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson outlines the basics and general flow of deploying applications and infrastructure in the cloud. Understanding the deployment pipeline (e.g., CI/CD) is essential for secure and efficient cloud operations.
learning_goals: Explain the stages of a typical cloud deployment pipeline (e.g., build, test, deploy).
Understand the role of Continuous Integration/Continuous Delivery (CI/CD) in cloud deployments.
Identify security considerations at each stage of the deployment flow.
Recognize the importance of automation and version control in cloud deployments.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Private + Understand the Concept of Serverless + Understand the basics and general flow of deploying in the cloud Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your understanding of Private Cloud characteristics, the Concept of Serverless computing and its security, and the general flow of deploying in the cloud. It ensures you can work with modern cloud architectures and deployment pipelines securely.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on private cloud benefits and deployment models.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of serverless computing security implications.
Understand the various stages and security considerations of cloud deployment workflows.
Achieve a passing score to progress in advanced cloud architecture and DevSecOps.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: S3
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers Amazon S3 (Simple Storage Service), a scalable object storage service offered by AWS. Understanding S3 security is crucial for managing data in the cloud, as misconfigurations are a common source of breaches.
learning_goals: Explain the purpose and functionality of AWS S3.
Understand S3 bucket policies, ACLs, and access control mechanisms.
Identify common S3 security misconfigurations and vulnerabilities.
Implement secure practices for storing and accessing data in S3 buckets.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Box
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module introduces Box, a cloud content management and file sharing service used by many organizations. Understanding Box security is important for securing enterprise data shared and stored in third-party cloud applications.
learning_goals: Understand the core functionalities of Box for file storage and collaboration.
Identify key security features and settings within Box.
Explain how to manage user access, sharing permissions, and external collaboration securely.
Recognize the importance of integrating Box with enterprise security controls.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Programming Skills
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module enhances and assesses your general Programming Skills, reinforcing fundamental coding concepts and introducing more advanced techniques relevant to cybersecurity. Strong programming skills are vital for automation, tool development, and script analysis.
learning_goals: Write efficient and readable code in at least one scripting language (e.g., Python).
Develop programs that interact with APIs and process data.
Implement basic security best practices in code (e.g., input validation, error handling).
Debug and troubleshoot code effectively.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: S3 + Box + Programming Skills Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your understanding of cloud storage security in AWS S3 and Box, alongside your general programming skills. It ensures you can secure data in cloud applications and leverage scripting for automation and security tasks.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on securing AWS S3 buckets and Box accounts.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of programming fundamentals and security implications.
Apply programming skills to interact with cloud services or analyze data.
Achieve a passing score to progress in cloud data security and secure development.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Google Drive
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module introduces Google Drive, a cloud-based file storage and synchronization service by Google. Understanding Google Drive security is essential as it's widely used for personal and business data storage.
learning_goals: Understand the core functionalities of Google Drive for file storage and sharing.
Identify key security and privacy settings within Google Drive.
Explain how to manage user access, sharing permissions, and external collaboration securely.
Recognize the importance of securing Google Drive accounts and data.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: HackTheBox
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers Hack The Box (HTB), an online platform for penetration testing training that provides various vulnerable machines (labs) to practice ethical hacking skills. HTB is crucial for practical, hands-on offensive security experience.
learning_goals: Utilize Hack The Box to practice penetration testing techniques on vulnerable machines.
Perform reconnaissance, exploitation, and privilege escalation in a controlled environment.
Develop problem-solving skills in offensive security scenarios.
Understand the importance of responsible disclosure and ethical hacking principles.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Security Engineer Project #26
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This project tasks you with securing cloud storage solutions (e.g., S3, Google Drive, Box) by configuring access controls, encryption, and monitoring. You will also use your programming skills to automate security checks or interact with cloud APIs securely.
learning_goals: Implement granular access controls and encryption for cloud storage buckets/folders.
Develop scripts to automate security auditing or data interaction with cloud storage APIs.
Demonstrate secure configuration of public and private cloud storage resources.
Validate the security posture of cloud storage through simulated access attempts or policy checks.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: TryHackMe
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module introduces TryHackMe (THM), an online platform providing guided cybersecurity training labs and challenges. THM is excellent for beginners and intermediate learners to gain practical skills across various security domains.
learning_goals: Utilize TryHackMe to follow guided labs and complete security challenges.
Learn and apply new cybersecurity concepts and tools in a structured environment.
Develop practical skills in areas like network security, web hacking, and forensics.
Track progress and expand knowledge through diverse learning paths.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: VulnHub
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers VulnHub, a platform that provides a wide range of downloadable vulnerable virtual machines for hands-on penetration testing practice. It's an excellent resource for honing ethical hacking skills in offline labs.
learning_goals: Download and set up vulnerable VMs from VulnHub in a virtualized environment.
Perform black-box penetration tests on various vulnerable systems.
Apply enumeration, exploitation, and privilege escalation techniques to compromise machines.
Document the steps taken to compromise a system and identify remediation.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: picoCTF
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module introduces picoCTF, a free online cybersecurity competition (Capture The Flag) aimed at middle and high school students, but suitable for all skill levels. picoCTF challenges cover a wide range of security topics in a gamified learning environment.
learning_goals: Participate in picoCTF challenges across various security categories (e.g., cryptography, web exploitation).
Develop problem-solving skills to decode puzzles and find flags.
Learn new tools and techniques through practical, bite-sized challenges.
Collaborate with peers to solve complex CTF problems.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: C#
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module introduces C#, a modern, object-oriented programming language developed by Microsoft. Learning C# is beneficial for security engineers working in Windows environments, developing secure applications, or analyzing .NET malware.
learning_goals: Understand C# syntax, data types, and control flow.
Develop simple applications or scripts using C#.
Familiarize yourself with the .NET framework and its security features.
Read and understand C# code for security analysis purposes.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: TryHackMe + VulnHub + picoCTF + C# Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your practical offensive security skills gained from platforms like TryHackMe, VulnHub, and picoCTF, alongside your understanding of the C# programming language. It ensures you have hands-on hacking experience and C# development/analysis capabilities.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions demonstrating knowledge from TryHackMe, VulnHub, and picoCTF challenges.
Demonstrate comprehensive understanding of C# syntax, object-oriented concepts, and its security relevance.
Apply C# programming basics to solve problems or analyze code.
Achieve a passing score to progress in advanced offensive security and secure software development.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: SANS Holiday Hack Challenge
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers the SANS Holiday Hack Challenge, an annual cybersecurity challenge and immersive learning experience. It provides practical scenarios across various security domains in a fun, gamified format.
learning_goals: Participate in the SANS Holiday Hack Challenge, solving cybersecurity puzzles and missions.
Apply skills in forensics, web exploitation, reverse engineering, and more in a simulated environment.
Learn new tools and techniques from SANS experts and community solutions.
Develop critical thinking and problem-solving skills under pressure.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Bluetooth
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module introduces Bluetooth technology, a wireless technology standard for exchanging data over short distances. Understanding Bluetooth security is crucial for assessing IoT and mobile device vulnerabilities.
learning_goals: Explain the basic operation and use cases of Bluetooth technology.
Identify common Bluetooth profiles and their functions.
Understand the security mechanisms in Bluetooth (e.g., pairing, encryption).
Recognize common Bluetooth vulnerabilities and attack vectors (e.g., Bluejacking, Bluesnarfing).

roadmap_checkpoints.label: SANS Holiday Hack Challenge + Bluetooth Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your participation and learning from the SANS Holiday Hack Challenge and your understanding of Bluetooth technology and its security implications. It ensures you have practical, diverse security experience and knowledge of wireless communication threats.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions related to challenges and concepts covered in the SANS Holiday Hack Challenge.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of Bluetooth operation, security, and vulnerabilities.
Identify common Bluetooth attacks and their mitigation strategies.
Achieve a passing score to progress in applied cybersecurity and wireless security.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: NFC
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module introduces Near Field Communication (NFC), a set of communication protocols for communication between two electronic devices over a distance of 4 cm or less. Understanding NFC security is crucial for mobile payments and access control systems.
learning_goals: Explain the basic operation and use cases of NFC technology.
Identify common NFC applications (e.g., mobile payments, access cards).
Understand the security mechanisms and limitations of NFC.
Recognize common NFC vulnerabilities and attack vectors (e.g., eavesdropping, data manipulation).

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Learn a Programming Language
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This checkpoint provides a reminder and opportunity to Learn a Programming Language, reinforcing that proficiency in at least one scripting or programming language is critical for security engineers. This is a continuous learning goal.
learning_goals: Select and commit to learning a programming language relevant to cybersecurity (e.g., Python, Go, C#).
Understand basic syntax, data structures, and control flow in the chosen language.
Write scripts to automate security tasks or develop simple security tools.
Read and debug code written in the chosen language.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Security Engineer Project #27
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This project tasks you with developing a small security tool or script using a programming language (e.g., C#, Python, Go) to automate a specific security task, analyze data, or interact with a security API. You'll also integrate concepts from wireless technologies like Bluetooth or NFC.
learning_goals: Design and develop a functional security tool/script using a chosen programming language.
Implement secure coding practices within your development.
Incorporate interaction with a wireless technology (Bluetooth/NFC) if applicable to the tool's function.
Demonstrate the tool's utility and provide documentation for its use.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Go
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module introduces Go (also known as Golang), an open-source programming language developed by Google. Go is gaining popularity in cybersecurity for developing high-performance network tools, reverse engineering, and cloud applications.
learning_goals: Understand Go language syntax, concurrency features, and standard library.
Develop simple command-line tools or network utilities in Go.
Familiarize yourself with Go's package management and build process.
Read and understand Go code for security analysis.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Infrared
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers Infrared (IR) technology, a wireless communication method that uses infrared light waves. While less common in general networking, it's relevant for specific short-range communication and remote controls, with security implications.
learning_goals: Explain the basic principles and use cases of infrared communication.
Understand the characteristics and limitations of IR technology (e.g., line-of-sight, short range).
Identify common applications of infrared in devices and systems.
Recognize potential security concerns and vulnerabilities related to IR communication.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: WiFi
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module provides a deep dive into WiFi (Wireless Fidelity) technology, covering its standards, infrastructure, and common security protocols. Understanding WiFi is essential for securing modern wireless networks.
learning_goals: Explain the fundamental principles of WiFi operation (e.g., 802.11 standards, access points).
Differentiate between various WiFi standards and their performance/security characteristics.
Understand common WiFi security protocols (e.g., WPA2, WPA3) and their implementation.
Identify common WiFi vulnerabilities and best practices for securing wireless networks.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: CompTIA A+
roadmap_checkpoints.description: The CompTIA A+ certification covers foundational IT skills, including hardware, networking, operating systems, and troubleshooting. It provides a baseline understanding of IT systems essential for a security engineer.
learning_goals: Assemble and configure computer hardware components.
Install and troubleshoot common operating systems (Windows, macOS, Linux).
Understand basic networking concepts and devices.
Apply systematic troubleshooting methodologies to IT issues.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Go + Infrared + WiFi + CompTIA A+ Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your understanding of the Go programming language, Infrared and WiFi wireless technologies, and the foundational IT knowledge covered by CompTIA A+. It ensures you have a blend of coding skills, wireless security awareness, and core IT expertise.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on Go programming syntax and concepts.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of Infrared and WiFi technologies, including security aspects.
Apply CompTIA A+ level knowledge to hardware, software, and networking scenarios.
Achieve a passing score to progress in secure development and comprehensive IT foundations.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: MS Office Suite
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module focuses on the Microsoft Office Suite, a collection of productivity software including Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Outlook. Understanding its features, common usage, and security settings is crucial, as Office documents are frequent attack vectors.
learning_goals: Master the core functionalities of Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Outlook.
Understand how macros can be used for automation and recognize their security risks.
Implement security settings within Office applications (e.g., macro security, trusted documents).
Identify common attack vectors targeting Office documents (e.g., phishing, malicious macros).

roadmap_checkpoints.label: CompTIA Network+
roadmap_checkpoints.description: The CompTIA Network+ certification covers foundational networking concepts, including network technologies, installation, configuration, management, and troubleshooting. It provides essential networking knowledge for a security engineer.
learning_goals: Explain network concepts like OSI model, TCP/IP, and various network topologies.
Configure and manage network devices such as routers, switches, and access points.
Implement basic network security controls.
Troubleshoot common network problems.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Operating System
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module provides a deeper understanding of Operating System (OS) principles beyond basic usage, covering memory management, process scheduling, file systems, and system calls. A strong grasp of OS internals is essential for low-level security analysis and hardening.
learning_goals: Understand the core components and architecture of an operating system.
Explain concepts like process management, threads, and virtual memory.
Differentiate between various file systems and their security implications.
Recognize the importance of system calls and kernel-level operations for security.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: MS Office Suite + CompTIA Network+ + Operating System Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your proficiency with the MS Office Suite, foundational networking concepts from CompTIA Network+, and advanced Operating System principles. It ensures you have practical application skills, strong network knowledge, and an understanding of OS internals for security.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on MS Office features, security settings, and common attack vectors.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of CompTIA Network+ topics, including network architecture and troubleshooting.
Explain advanced operating system concepts and their security relevance.
Achieve a passing score to progress in system-level security and network design.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Windows
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module focuses on the Windows operating system, covering its architecture, administration, security features, and common vulnerabilities. Deep knowledge of Windows is essential for securing enterprise environments.
learning_goals: Understand Windows architecture, including kernel, user mode, and APIs.
Perform advanced Windows administration tasks (e.g., Registry editing, Group Policy).
Identify and configure Windows security features (e.g., Defender, UAC, BitLocker).
Recognize common Windows vulnerabilities and exploitation techniques.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: iCloud
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson introduces iCloud, Apple's cloud storage and computing service. Understanding iCloud's security features and potential vulnerabilities is important for securing Apple ecosystems and protecting personal data.
learning_goals: Explain the purpose and core functionalities of iCloud.
Identify key security and privacy settings within iCloud.
Understand how to secure iCloud accounts and data, including MFA.
Recognize common iCloud-related security concerns (e.g., phishing, account compromise).

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Security Engineer Project #28
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This project tasks you with hardening a Windows workstation, implementing advanced security configurations, and integrating secure cloud storage practices using services like iCloud or other personal cloud storage. You'll demonstrate comprehensive endpoint and cloud data security.
learning_goals: Apply advanced hardening techniques to a Windows operating system, including Group Policy and local security settings.
Configure and secure cloud storage (e.g., iCloud) with strong authentication and data encryption.
Implement monitoring for suspicious activities on the Windows endpoint.
Document the hardening steps and cloud security configurations, justifying choices.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: CompTIA Security+
roadmap_checkpoints.description: The CompTIA Security+ certification is a global certification that validates the baseline skills necessary to perform core security functions and pursue an IT security career. It covers general security concepts, threats, vulnerabilities, and cryptography.
learning_goals: Understand core cybersecurity concepts, threats, attacks, and vulnerabilities.
Implement security controls for network, host, and application layers.
Explain identity and access management principles.
Apply risk management and incident response concepts.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Bash
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module focuses on Bash scripting, a powerful tool for automating tasks and managing systems in Unix-like operating systems. Proficiency in Bash is essential for Linux administration, security automation, and forensic tasks.
learning_goals: Write basic and intermediate Bash scripts for task automation.
Understand Bash syntax, variables, conditionals, and loops.
Automate system administration, file manipulation, and security checks.
Debug Bash scripts and handle errors effectively.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Google Suite
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers the Google Suite (now Google Workspace), a collection of cloud computing, productivity, and collaboration tools. Understanding its security features and potential vulnerabilities is crucial for securing enterprise cloud collaboration.
learning_goals: Understand the core functionalities of Google Workspace applications (e.g., Gmail, Docs, Drive, Meet).
Identify key security and privacy settings within Google Workspace.
Explain how to manage user access, sharing permissions, and external collaboration securely.
Recognize the importance of securing Google Workspace accounts and data against common threats.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: CEH
roadmap_checkpoints.description: The CEH (Certified Ethical Hacker) certification validates an individual's skills in ethical hacking techniques and methodologies. It covers various attack vectors, tools, and countermeasures, focusing on the attacker's perspective.
learning_goals: Understand ethical hacking phases (reconnaissance, scanning, enumeration, exploitation).
Utilize common ethical hacking tools and techniques across different domains.
Identify system and network vulnerabilities and develop attack strategies.
Explain defensive countermeasures against common cyberattacks.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: CompTIA Security+ + Bash + Google Suite + CEH Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your foundational security knowledge from CompTIA Security+, your proficiency in Bash scripting, your understanding of Google Suite security, and your ethical hacking skills from CEH. It ensures a strong blend of defensive and offensive capabilities.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on CompTIA Security+ concepts, threats, and controls.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of Bash scripting for automation and system management.
Explain Google Suite security features and best practices for secure collaboration.
Apply CEH ethical hacking methodologies to identify and mitigate vulnerabilities.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Linux
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module delves into the Linux operating system, covering its architecture, various distributions, command-line interface, system administration, and security features. Deep knowledge of Linux is essential for server security and open-source environments.
learning_goals: Understand Linux kernel architecture, user space, and shell environment.
Master advanced Linux command-line tools and utilities.
Perform comprehensive Linux system administration tasks (e.g., user management, service control).
Identify and configure Linux security features (e.g., SELinux, AppArmor, auditing).

roadmap_checkpoints.label: MacOS
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson focuses on the macOS operating system, covering its Unix-like foundation, security features, and unique administration aspects. Understanding macOS security is important for securing Apple endpoints in enterprise and personal use.
learning_goals: Understand macOS architecture, including Darwin kernel and user interface.
Perform macOS system administration and troubleshooting tasks.
Identify and configure macOS security features (e.g., Gatekeeper, FileVault, T2 chip security).
Recognize common macOS vulnerabilities and best practices for hardening.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: GSEC
roadmap_checkpoints.description: The GIAC Security Essentials Certification (GSEC) validates a practitioner's understanding of information security beyond simple terminology and concepts. It focuses on hands-on practical skills in areas like network security, cryptography, and incident handling.
learning_goals: Apply foundational security principles to real-world scenarios.
Implement and manage network security controls.
Understand cryptographic concepts and their applications.
Perform basic incident response and digital forensics tasks.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Linux + MacOS + GSEC Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your deep understanding of Linux and macOS operating systems, including their administration and security features, alongside the practical security knowledge validated by GSEC. It ensures strong foundational skills across major OS platforms.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on Linux system administration, security, and hardening.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of macOS architecture, security features, and best practices.
Apply GSEC-level practical security skills in network, cryptography, and incident handling.
Achieve a passing score to progress in cross-platform security and practical defense.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Terminal Knowledge
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module provides comprehensive Terminal Knowledge, focusing on advanced command-line usage, scripting, and efficient interaction with operating systems. Mastery of the terminal is essential for system administration, automation, and security analysis.
learning_goals: Master advanced command-line navigation and file manipulation.
Utilize shell scripting for complex automation tasks.
Understand regular expressions and advanced text processing utilities.
Optimize terminal workflows for efficiency in various security operations.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Power Shell
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson focuses on PowerShell, a powerful command-line shell and scripting language developed by Microsoft for task automation and configuration management in Windows environments. Proficiency in PowerShell is crucial for Windows security and administration.
learning_goals: Write basic and intermediate PowerShell scripts for Windows automation.
Understand PowerShell cmdlets, pipeline, and object-oriented nature.
Automate Windows system administration, security auditing, and forensics tasks.
Debug PowerShell scripts and handle errors effectively.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: GIAC
roadmap_checkpoints.description: GIAC (Global Information Assurance Certification) is a leading provider of information security certifications, focusing on practical and hands-on skills. This module broadly refers to the pursuit of GIAC certifications to validate expertise in various specialized security domains.
learning_goals: Understand the range of specialized GIAC certifications available.
Identify specific GIAC certifications relevant to chosen career path (e.g., forensics, pen testing, cloud).
Prepare for and pursue a GIAC certification to validate practical skills.
Apply GIAC-level knowledge and methodologies in real-world security scenarios.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Security Engineer Project #29
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This project tasks you with automating complex security and system administration tasks across both Linux and Windows environments using Bash and PowerShell scripting, respectively. You'll demonstrate advanced terminal knowledge and cross-platform scripting capabilities.
learning_goals: Develop robust Bash scripts for Linux system hardening, log analysis, or automated security checks.
Create powerful PowerShell scripts for Windows security auditing, user management, or incident response tasks.
Implement error handling and logging within scripts for reliability.
Demonstrate efficient use of terminal commands and scripting for cross-platform automation.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: CISSP
roadmap_checkpoints.description: The CISSP (Certified Information Systems Security Professional) is a globally recognized certification that validates an information security professional's ability to design, implement, and manage a best-in-class cybersecurity program. It covers eight domains of security.
learning_goals: Understand the eight domains of the CISSP Common Body of Knowledge (CBK).
Apply security governance, risk management, and compliance principles.
Design secure architectures and engineering solutions.
Manage security operations, incident response, and software development security.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: DNS
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module provides an in-depth understanding of the Domain Name System (DNS), including its hierarchy, resolution process, record types, and security considerations. Deep knowledge of DNS is crucial for network communication and preventing DNS-based attacks.
learning_goals: Explain the hierarchy and resolution process of the DNS system.
Differentiate between various DNS record types (A, AAAA, CNAME, MX, NS, SOA).
Understand common DNS vulnerabilities (e.g., cache poisoning, DDoS) and mitigation.
Configure and troubleshoot DNS services for secure operation.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: CISSP + DNS Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your comprehensive understanding of the CISSP domains for security management and architecture, alongside an in-depth knowledge of the Domain Name System (DNS). It ensures you have high-level strategic security expertise and critical network protocol mastery.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions across all eight CISSP domains.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of DNS hierarchy, record types, and security best practices.
Apply strategic security management principles to organizational scenarios.
Achieve a passing score to signify expert-level security leadership and technical understanding.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: SSL / TLS
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module provides a deeper dive into SSL/TLS protocols, covering their cryptographic foundations, handshake process, certificate validation, and advanced configuration options. Mastering SSL/TLS is crucial for securing web traffic and network communications.
learning_goals: Explain the cryptographic primitives underlying SSL/TLS (e.g., symmetric/asymmetric encryption, hashing).
Understand the detailed SSL/TLS handshake process and session negotiation.
Differentiate between various TLS versions and cipher suites, identifying secure configurations.
Configure and troubleshoot SSL/TLS implementations for web servers and applications.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: SSH
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers SSH (Secure Shell), a cryptographic network protocol for operating network services securely over an unsecured network. SSH is widely used for secure remote access, file transfer, and command execution.
learning_goals: Explain the purpose and cryptographic mechanisms of SSH.
Understand how SSH provides secure remote access and command-line interface.
Differentiate between password-based and key-based SSH authentication.
Implement secure SSH configurations and manage SSH keys effectively.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: AWS
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module introduces AWS (Amazon Web Services), the world's most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. Understanding AWS security is crucial for securing cloud infrastructure, applications, and data.
learning_goals: Understand the core security services and best practices in AWS (e.g., IAM, Security Groups, VPC).
Explain the AWS shared responsibility model and its implications.
Identify common AWS security vulnerabilities and misconfigurations.
Configure fundamental AWS security controls for compute, storage, and networking services.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Azure
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers Azure (Microsoft Azure), a growing collection of integrated cloud services used for building, deploying, and managing applications and services. Understanding Azure security is essential for hybrid and Microsoft-centric cloud environments.
learning_goals: Understand the core security services and best practices in Azure (e.g., Azure AD, Network Security Groups).
Explain the Azure shared responsibility model and its implications.
Identify common Azure security vulnerabilities and misconfigurations.
Configure fundamental Azure security controls for compute, storage, and networking services.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: SSL / TLS + SSH + AWS + Azure Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your advanced understanding of SSL/TLS and SSH for secure communication, and your knowledge of security best practices in leading cloud platforms: AWS and Azure. It ensures you can secure data in transit and manage cloud security effectively.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on advanced SSL/TLS configurations and troubleshooting.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of SSH security, authentication, and key management.
Apply AWS and Azure security services and best practices to cloud infrastructure.
Achieve a passing score to signify expertise in secure protocols and multi-cloud security.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Whaling
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers Whaling, a highly targeted phishing attack (spear phishing) aimed at senior executives or high-profile individuals within an organization. Understanding whaling is crucial for protecting organizational leadership and sensitive data.
learning_goals: Define whaling and differentiate it from other phishing attacks.
Understand the motivations and sophisticated techniques used in whaling attacks.
Identify the high-impact consequences of a successful whaling attack.
Implement defensive strategies, including executive awareness training and robust email security, against whaling.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Whishing
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module introduces Whishing (or vishing), a social engineering attack that uses voice communication (phone calls, voicemail) to trick victims into revealing sensitive information or performing actions. Understanding whishing is vital for protecting against voice-based deception.
learning_goals: Define whishing and recognize its characteristics as a voice-based phishing attack.
Identify common tactics used in whishing attacks (e.g., impersonating support, bank, or government officials).
Understand the psychological manipulation involved in voice scams.
Implement strategies to prevent and mitigate whishing attacks (e.g., employee training, call verification).

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Security Engineer Project #30
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This project tasks you with implementing secure access controls for cloud resources on both AWS and Azure platforms, ensuring robust authentication and authorization. You'll also integrate advanced email and communication security to defend against whaling and whishing attacks.
learning_goals: Configure granular IAM policies and roles for secure access to AWS and Azure resources.
Implement multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all cloud administrative accounts.
Develop and test email security configurations to detect and block whaling attempts.
Design user awareness training to combat whishing and other sophisticated social engineering attacks.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Spam vs Spim
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson differentiates between Spam and Spim, both forms of unsolicited messages, but delivered through different communication channels. Understanding these differences helps in implementing appropriate filtering and defense mechanisms.
learning_goals: Define spam as unsolicited bulk email messages.
Define spim as unsolicited commercial instant messages.
Understand the common goals of spammers and spimmers (e.g., advertising, malware distribution).
Identify methods for filtering and blocking spam and spim.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: tail
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module covers the 'tail' command, a Unix/Linux utility used to display the last few lines of a text file. It's particularly useful for monitoring log files in real-time or quickly checking recent entries.
learning_goals: Use 'tail' to display the end of a file.
Specify the number of lines or bytes to display.
Utilize the '-f' option to monitor file changes in real-time.
Quickly review recent entries in system logs or application output.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Spam vs Spim + tail Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your understanding of Spam vs. Spim for message filtering and your proficiency with the 'tail' command for real-time log monitoring. It ensures you can combat unsolicited messages and efficiently analyze dynamic log files.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on the differences and mitigation of spam and spim.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of using 'tail' to view file endings and monitor logs.
Apply 'tail' with the '-f' option for real-time security event monitoring.
Achieve a passing score to progress in communication security and log analysis.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Tailgating
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers Tailgating, a physical social engineering technique where an unauthorized person gains entry to a restricted area by following an authorized person closely. It highlights the importance of physical access control.
learning_goals: Define tailgating and explain its mechanism in physical security.
Understand how tailgaters exploit social norms to bypass access controls.
Identify the risks associated with unauthorized physical access.
Implement countermeasures to prevent tailgating (e.g., security awareness, turnstiles, strict access policies).

roadmap_checkpoints.label: curl
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module introduces 'curl', a command-line tool for transferring data with URLs, supporting a wide range of protocols including HTTP, HTTPS, FTP, and more. 'curl' is essential for interacting with web services and testing network connectivity.
learning_goals: Utilize 'curl' to send HTTP requests and retrieve web content.
Perform various 'curl' operations (e.g., GET, POST, custom headers, cookies).
Understand how 'curl' interacts with different protocols and handles authentication.
Use 'curl' for testing web APIs, troubleshooting network issues, and security assessments.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: FTK Imager
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson introduces FTK Imager, a free data preview and imaging tool by AccessData. It's widely used in digital forensics to create forensic images of hard drives, volatile memory, and other data sources while preserving evidence integrity.
learning_goals: Understand the purpose and capabilities of FTK Imager in digital forensics.
Utilize FTK Imager to create forensic images of physical drives, logical volumes, and memory.
Verify the integrity of forensic images using hashing.
Recognize the importance of chain of custody and evidence preservation during imaging.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Impersonation
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module covers Impersonation, a social engineering technique where an attacker pretends to be someone else to gain information or access. Understanding impersonation is crucial for recognizing and defending against identity-based deception.
learning_goals: Define impersonation and identify its various forms (e.g., in person, via phone/email).
Understand the psychological tactics used by impersonators to build trust or authority.
Recognize indicators of impersonation attempts.
Implement defensive strategies, including verification protocols and security awareness, against impersonation.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Tailgating + curl + FTK Imager + Impersonation Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your understanding of physical security threats like Tailgating, your proficiency with the 'curl' command for web interaction, your knowledge of forensic imaging with FTK Imager, and the social engineering technique of Impersonation. It ensures you can address diverse attack vectors.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on preventing and mitigating tailgating attacks.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of using 'curl' for web requests and API testing.
Apply FTK Imager for creating forensically sound data images.
Understand impersonation tactics and implement effective defense mechanisms.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Event Logs
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module covers Event Logs, records of events that occur on a system or application, such as user logins, program errors, and security-related actions. Analyzing event logs is fundamental for security monitoring, auditing, and incident investigation.
learning_goals: Understand the different types of event logs (e.g., Security, System, Application) in various OSs.
Explain the key information contained within event log entries (e.g., event ID, source, time, user).
Utilize event log viewers or analysis tools to review and filter logs.
Identify suspicious or malicious activities by analyzing event log patterns.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Packet Captures
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson introduces Packet Captures, which are recordings of network traffic, often in the form of PCAP files. Analyzing packet captures is a critical skill for network troubleshooting, security analysis, and digital forensics.
learning_goals: Explain the purpose and value of packet captures in network analysis.
Understand how packet captures are created and stored.
Utilize tools (e.g., Wireshark) to open and analyze PCAP files.
Identify network anomalies, malicious payloads, and protocol errors within packet captures.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: MITM
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module covers Man-in-the-Middle (MITM) attacks, where an attacker secretly relays and possibly alters the communication between two parties who believe they are directly communicating with each other. Understanding MITM is crucial for securing network communications.
learning_goals: Define a Man-in-the-Middle (MITM) attack and its underlying mechanism.
Identify common MITM attack vectors (e.g., ARP spoofing, DNS spoofing, rogue APs).
Understand how MITM attacks compromise confidentiality and integrity.
Implement defensive measures against MITM attacks (e.g., strong encryption, mutual authentication, network segmentation).

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Security Engineer Project #31
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This project tasks you with detecting and analyzing a simulated Man-in-the-Middle (MITM) attack using event logs and packet captures. You will identify the attack's stages, collect evidence, and propose remediation strategies.
learning_goals: Set up a scenario to simulate a Man-in-the-Middle attack.
Utilize event logs to detect suspicious activities indicative of an MITM attack.
Capture and analyze network packets (PCAP) to identify the MITM mechanism and compromised data.
Document the attack timeline, evidence collected, and proposed defensive measures against MITM.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: CSRF
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF), a web security vulnerability that tricks a victim's web browser into performing unwanted actions on a web application where they are currently authenticated. Understanding CSRF is critical for web application security.
learning_goals: Define CSRF and explain its mechanism.
Understand how an attacker can craft malicious requests that a victim's browser will execute.
Identify the impact of successful CSRF attacks (e.g., unauthorized transactions, data changes).
Implement defensive measures to prevent CSRF (e.g., anti-CSRF tokens, SameSite cookies).

roadmap_checkpoints.label: XSS
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module introduces Cross-Site Scripting (XSS), a type of security vulnerability typically found in web applications that enables attackers to inject client-side scripts into web pages viewed by other users. XSS is a prevalent web vulnerability.
learning_goals: Define XSS and differentiate between its types (e.g., reflected, stored, DOM-based).
Understand how attackers inject malicious scripts into web pages.
Identify the impact of successful XSS attacks (e.g., session hijacking, defacement).
Implement defensive measures to prevent XSS (e.g., input validation, output encoding, Content Security Policy).

roadmap_checkpoints.label: CSRF + XSS Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your understanding of Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) and Cross-Site Scripting (XSS), two critical web application vulnerabilities. It ensures you can identify, exploit (ethically), and mitigate these common threats to web services.
learning_goals: Successfully answer questions on the mechanisms and impact of CSRF attacks.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of XSS types, exploitation, and prevention.
Identify input and output handling issues that lead to CSRF and XSS.
Achieve a passing score to progress in web application security and secure coding.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: SQL Injection
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers SQL Injection, a web security vulnerability that allows an attacker to interfere with the queries that an application makes to its database. SQL Injection is a severe and common vulnerability that can lead to data theft or full system compromise.
learning_goals: Define SQL Injection and explain its mechanism.
Understand how attackers can manipulate SQL queries through user input.
Differentiate between various types of SQL Injection (e.g., error-based, union-based, blind).
Implement defensive measures to prevent SQL Injection (e.g., parameterized queries, input validation).

roadmap_checkpoints.label: MAC-based
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module introduces MAC-based (Media Access Control) security, which involves filtering network access based on the MAC addresses of devices. While simple, it has limitations and is generally part of a layered security approach.
learning_goals: Explain the concept of MAC address filtering for network access control.
Understand how MAC addresses are used to identify devices on a local network.
Identify the strengths (e.g., simple to implement) and weaknesses (e.g., MAC spoofing) of MAC-based security.
Recognize scenarios where MAC filtering might be a useful, albeit limited, security control.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: VLAN Hopping
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This lesson covers VLAN Hopping, a network attack that allows an attacker on one VLAN to gain access to traffic on other VLANs that would normally be inaccessible. Understanding VLAN hopping is crucial for securing segmented networks.
learning_goals: Define VLAN hopping and explain its two primary methods (switch spoofing, double tagging).
